   673→func (c *Context) planWalk(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   674→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   675→	log.Printf("[DEBUG] Building and walking plan graph for %s", opts.Mode)
   676→
   677→	prevRunState = prevRunState.DeepCopy() // don't modify the caller's object when we process the moves
   678→	moveStmts, moveResults, moveDiags := c.prePlanFindAndApplyMoves(config, prevRunState)
   679→	diags = diags.Append(moveDiags)
   680→	if moveDiags.HasErrors() {
   681→		return nil, nil, diags
   682→	}
   683→
   684→	// If resource targeting is in effect then it might conflict with the
   685→	// move result.
   686→	diags = diags.Append(c.prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults, opts.Targets))
   687→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   688→		// We'll return early here, because if we have any moved resource
   689→		// instances excluded by targeting then planning is likely to encounter
   690→		// strange problems that may lead to confusing error messages.
   691→		return nil, nil, diags
   692→	}
   693→
   694→	graph, walkOp, moreDiags := c.planGraph(config, prevRunState, opts)
   695→	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   696→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   697→		return nil, nil, diags
   698→	}
   699→
   700→	timestamp := time.Now().UTC()
   701→	if opts.ForcePlanTimestamp != nil {
   702→		// Some tests use this to produce stable results to assert against.
   703→		timestamp = *opts.ForcePlanTimestamp
   704→	}
   705→
   706→	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   707→	if opts != nil {
   708→		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   709→	}
   710→
   711→	// If we get here then we should definitely have a non-nil "graph", which
   712→	// we can now walk.
   713→	changes := plans.NewChanges()
   714→
   715→	// Initialize the results table to validate provider function calls.
   716→	// Hold reference to this so we can store the table data in the plan file.
   717→	providerFuncResults := providers.NewFunctionResultsTable(nil)
   718→
   719→	walker, walkDiags := c.walk(graph, walkOp, &graphWalkOpts{
   720→		Config:                     config,
   721→		InputState:                 prevRunState,
   722→		ExternalProviderConfigs:    externalProviderConfigs,
   723→		DeferralAllowed:            opts.DeferralAllowed,
   724→		ExternalDependencyDeferred: opts.ExternalDependencyDeferred,
   725→		Changes:                    changes,
   726→		MoveResults:                moveResults,
   727→		Overrides:                  opts.Overrides,
   728→		PlanTimeTimestamp:          timestamp,
   729→		ProviderFuncResults:        providerFuncResults,
   730→		Forget:                     opts.Forget,
   731→	})
   732→	diags = diags.Append(walker.NonFatalDiagnostics)
   733→	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   734→
   735→	allInsts := walker.InstanceExpander.AllInstances()
   736→
   737→	moveValidateDiags := c.postPlanValidateMoves(config, moveStmts, allInsts)
   738→	if moveValidateDiags.HasErrors() {
   739→		// If any of the move statements are invalid then those errors take
   740→		// precedence over any other errors because an incomplete move graph
   741→		// is quite likely to be the _cause_ of various errors. This oddity
   742→		// comes from the fact that we need to apply the moves before we
   743→		// actually validate them, because validation depends on the result
   744→		// of first trying to plan.
   745→		return nil, nil, moveValidateDiags
   746→	}
   747→	diags = diags.Append(moveValidateDiags) // might just contain warnings
   748→
   749→	if moveResults.Blocked.Len() > 0 && !diags.HasErrors() {
   750→		// If we had blocked moves and we're not going to be returning errors
   751→		// then we'll report the blockers as a warning. We do this only in the
   752→		// absense of errors because invalid move statements might well be
   753→		// the root cause of the blockers, and so better to give an actionable
   754→		// error message than a less-actionable warning.
   755→		diags = diags.Append(blockedMovesWarningDiag(moveResults))
   756→	}
   757→
   758→	// If we reach this point with error diagnostics then "changes" is a
   759→	// representation of the subset of changes we were able to plan before
   760→	// we encountered errors, which we'll return as part of a non-nil plan
   761→	// so that e.g. the UI can show what was planned so far in case that extra
   762→	// context helps the user to understand the error messages we're returning.
   763→	prevRunState = walker.PrevRunState.Close()
   764→
   765→	// The refreshed state may have data resource objects which were deferred
   766→	// to apply and cannot be serialized.
   767→	walker.RefreshState.RemovePlannedResourceInstanceObjects()
   768→	priorState := walker.RefreshState.Close()
   769→
   770→	driftedResources, driftDiags := c.driftedResources(config, prevRunState, priorState, moveResults)
   771→	diags = diags.Append(driftDiags)
   772→
   773→	deferredResources, deferredDiags := c.deferredResources(config, walker.Deferrals.GetDeferredChanges(), priorState)
   774→	diags = diags.Append(deferredDiags)
   775→
   776→	var forgottenResources []string
   777→	for _, rc := range changes.Resources {
   778→		if rc.Action == plans.Forget {
   779→			// TODO KEM display resource ids
   780→			forgottenResources = append(forgottenResources, fmt.Sprintf(" - %s", rc.Addr))
   781→		}
   782→	}
   783→	if len(forgottenResources) > 0 {
   784→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   785→			tfdiags.Warning,
   786→			"Some objects will no longer be managed by Terraform",
   787→			fmt.Sprintf("If you apply this plan, Terraform will discard its tracking information for the following objects, but it will not delete them:\n%s\n\nAfter applying this plan, Terraform will no longer manage these objects. You will need to import them into Terraform to manage them again.", strings.Join(forgottenResources, "\n")),
   788→		))
   789→	}
   790→	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, prevRunState)
   791→	// We must finish building a plan object, and cannot return early here.
   792→	var changesSrc *plans.ChangesSrc
   793→	var err error
   794→	if !schemaDiags.HasErrors() {
   795→		changesSrc, err = changes.Encode(schemas)
   796→		if err != nil {
   797→			diags = diags.Append(err)
   798→		}
   799→	}
   800→
   801→	plan := &plans.Plan{
   802→		UIMode:                  opts.Mode,
   803→		Changes:                 changesSrc,
   804→		DriftedResources:        driftedResources,
   805→		DeferredResources:       deferredResources,
   806→		PrevRunState:            prevRunState,
   807→		PriorState:              priorState,
   808→		ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   809→		Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   810→		Checks:                  states.NewCheckResults(walker.Checks),
   811→		Timestamp:               timestamp,
   812→		ProviderFunctionResults: providerFuncResults.GetHashes(),
   813→
   814→		// Other fields get populated by Context.Plan after we return
   815→	}
   816→
   817→	// Our final rulings on whether the plan is "complete" and "applyable".
   818→	// See the documentation for these plan fields to learn what exactly they
   819→	// are intended to mean.
   820→	if !diags.HasErrors() {
   821→		if len(opts.Targets) == 0 && !walker.Deferrals.HaveAnyDeferrals() {
   822→			// A plan without any targets or deferred actions should be
   823→			// complete if we didn't encounter errors while producing it.
   824→			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is complete")
   825→			plan.Complete = true
   826→		} else {
   827→			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is incomplete")
   828→		}
   829→		if opts.Mode == plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   830→			// In refresh-only mode we explicitly don't expect to propose any
   831→			// actions, but the plan is applyable if the state was changed
   832→			// in an interesting way by the refresh step.
   833→			plan.Applyable = !plan.PriorState.ManagedResourcesEqual(plan.PrevRunState) || !plan.PriorState.RootOutputValuesEqual(plan.PrevRunState)
   834→		} else {
   835→			// For other planning modes a plan is applyable if its "changes"
   836→			// are not considered empty (by whatever rules the plans package
   837→			// uses to decide that).
   838→			plan.Applyable = !plan.Changes.Empty()
   839→		}
   840→		if plan.Applyable {
   841→			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is applyable")
   842→		} else {
   843→			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is not applyable")
   844→		}
   845→	} else {
   846→		log.Println("[WARN] Planning encountered errors, so plan is not applyable")
   847→	}
   848→
   849→	// The caller also gets access to an expression evaluation scope in the
   850→	// root module, in case it needs to extract other information using
   851→	// expressions, like in "terraform console" or the test harness.
   852→	evalScope := evalScopeFromGraphWalk(walker, addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   853→
   854→	return plan, evalScope, diags
   855→}

   887→func (c *Context) planGraph(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*Graph, walkOperation, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   888→	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   889→	if opts != nil {
   890→		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   891→	}
   892→
   893→	switch mode := opts.Mode; mode {
   894→	case plans.NormalMode:
   895→		// In Normal mode we need to pay attention to import and removed blocks
   896→		// in config so their targets can be added to the graph.
   897→		forgetResources, forgetModules, diags := c.findForgetTargets(config)
   898→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   899→			return nil, walkPlan, diags
   900→		}
   901→		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   902→			Config:                  config,
   903→			State:                   prevRunState,
   904→			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   905→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   906→			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   907→			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   908→			ForceReplace:            opts.ForceReplace,
   909→			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   910→			preDestroyRefresh:       opts.PreDestroyRefresh,
   911→			Operation:               walkPlan,
   912→			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   913→			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   914→			ImportTargets:           c.findImportTargets(config),
   915→			forgetResources:         forgetResources,
   916→			forgetModules:           forgetModules,
   917→			GenerateConfigPath:      opts.GenerateConfigPath,
   918→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   919→		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   920→	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   921→		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   922→			Config:                  config,
   923→			State:                   prevRunState,
   924→			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   925→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   926→			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   927→			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   928→			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   929→			skipPlanChanges:         true, // this activates "refresh only" mode.
   930→			Operation:               walkPlan,
   931→			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   932→			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   933→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   934→		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   935→	case plans.DestroyMode:
   936→		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   937→			Config:                  config,
   938→			State:                   prevRunState,
   939→			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   940→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   941→			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   942→			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   943→			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   944→			Operation:               walkPlanDestroy,
   945→			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   946→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   947→		return graph, walkPlanDestroy, diags
   948→	default:
   949→		// The above should cover all plans.Mode values
   950→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported plan mode %s", mode))
   951→	}
   952→}

   887→func (c *Context) planGraph(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*Graph, walkOperation, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   888→	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   889→	if opts != nil {

   894→	case plans.NormalMode:
   895→		// In Normal mode we need to pay attention to import and removed blocks
   896→		// in config so their targets can be added to the graph.

   907→			Targets:                 opts.Targets,

   910→			preDestroyRefresh:       opts.PreDestroyRefresh,
   911→			Operation:               walkPlan,
   912→			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   913→			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   914→			ImportTargets:           c.findImportTargets(config),
   915→			forgetResources:         forgetResources,
   916→			forgetModules:           forgetModules,
   917→			GenerateConfigPath:      opts.GenerateConfigPath,
   918→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   919→		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   920→	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
   921→		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
   922→			Config:                  config,
   923→			State:                   prevRunState,
   924→			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,
   925→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   926→			Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   927→			Targets:                 opts.Targets,
   928→			skipRefresh:             opts.SkipRefresh,
   929→			skipPlanChanges:         true, // this activates "refresh only" mode.
   930→			Operation:               walkPlan,
   931→			ExternalReferences:      opts.ExternalReferences,
   932→			Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   933→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   934→		return graph, walkPlan, diags
   935→	case plans.DestroyMode:
   936→		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{

   939→			RootVariableValues:      opts.SetVariables,

   946→		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)

   948→	default:
   949→		// The above should cover all plans.Mode values
   950→		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported plan mode %s", mode))
   951→	}
   952→}

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"log"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for
    19→// planning and for other "plan-like" operations which don't require an
    20→// already-calculated plan as input.
    21→//
    22→// Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:
    23→//
    24→//   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which
    25→//     represents the desired state.
    26→//
    27→//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because
    28→//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning
    29→//     to take against a particular resource instance.
    30→type PlanGraphBuilder struct {
    31→	// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.
    32→	Config *configs.Config
    33→
    34→	// State is the current state
    35→	State *states.State
    36→
    37→	// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables
    38→	// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part
    39→	// of the plan walk.
    40→	RootVariableValues InputValues
    41→
    42→	// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider
    43→	// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available
    44→	// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit
    45→	// initialization step.
    46→	ExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
    47→
    48→	// Plugins is a library of plug-in components (providers and
    49→	// provisioners) available for use.
    50→	Plugins *contextPlugins
    51→
    52→	// Targets are resources to target
    53→	Targets []addrs.Targetable
    54→
    55→	// ForceReplace are resource instances where if we would normally have
    56→	// generated a NoOp or Update action then we'll force generating a replace
    57→	// action instead. Create and Delete actions are not affected.
    58→	ForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    59→
    60→	// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing managed resources
    61→	skipRefresh bool
    62→
    63→	// preDestroyRefresh indicates that we are executing the refresh which
    64→	// happens immediately before a destroy plan, which happens to use the
    65→	// normal planing mode so skipPlanChanges cannot be set.
    66→	preDestroyRefresh bool
    67→
    68→	// skipPlanChanges indicates that we should skip the step of comparing
    69→	// prior state with configuration and generating planned changes to
    70→	// resource instances. (This is for the "refresh only" planning mode,
    71→	// where we _only_ do the refresh step.)
    72→	skipPlanChanges bool
    73→
    74→	ConcreteProvider                ConcreteProviderNodeFunc
    75→	ConcreteResource                ConcreteResourceNodeFunc
    76→	ConcreteResourceInstance        ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    77→	ConcreteResourceOrphan          ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    78→	ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc
    79→	ConcreteModule                  ConcreteModuleNodeFunc
    80→
    81→	// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.
    82→	Operation walkOperation
    83→
    84→	// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to
    85→	// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within
    86→	// the actual graph.
    87→	ExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference
    88→
    89→	// Overrides provides the set of overrides supplied by the testing
    90→	// framework.
    91→	Overrides *mocking.Overrides
    92→
    93→	// ImportTargets are the list of resources to import.
    94→	ImportTargets []*ImportTarget
    95→
    96→	// forgetResources lists the resources that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed
    97→	// from state without destroying.
    98→	forgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource
    99→
   100→	// forgetModules lists the modules that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed
   101→	// from state without destroying.
   102→	forgetModules []addrs.Module
   103→
   104→	// GenerateConfig tells Terraform where to write and generated config for
   105→	// any import targets that do not already have configuration.
   106→	//
   107→	// If empty, then config will not be generated.
   108→	GenerateConfigPath string
   109→}
   110→
   111→// See GraphBuilder
   112→func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   113→	log.Printf("[TRACE] building graph for %s", b.Operation)
   114→	return (&BasicGraphBuilder{
   115→		Steps: b.Steps(),
   116→		Name:  "PlanGraphBuilder",
   117→	}).Build(path)
   118→}
   119→
   120→// See GraphBuilder
   121→func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {
   122→	switch b.Operation {
   123→	case walkPlan:
   124→		b.initPlan()
   125→	case walkPlanDestroy:
   126→		b.initDestroy()
   127→	case walkValidate:
   128→		b.initValidate()
   129→	case walkImport:
   130→		b.initImport()
   131→	default:
   132→		panic("invalid plan operation: " + b.Operation.String())
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	steps := []GraphTransformer{
   136→		// Creates all the resources represented in the config
   137→		&ConfigTransformer{
   138→			Concrete: b.ConcreteResource,
   139→			Config:   b.Config,
   140→			destroy:  b.Operation == walkDestroy || b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,
   141→
   142→			importTargets: b.ImportTargets,
   143→
   144→			// We only want to generate config during a plan operation.
   145→			generateConfigPathForImportTargets: b.GenerateConfigPath,
   146→		},
   147→
   148→		// Add dynamic values
   149→		&RootVariableTransformer{
   150→			Config:       b.Config,
   151→			RawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,
   152→			Planning:     true,
   153→			DestroyApply: false, // always false for planning
   154→		},
   155→		&ModuleVariableTransformer{
   156→			Config:       b.Config,
   157→			Planning:     true,
   158→			DestroyApply: false, // always false for planning
   159→		},
   160→		&variableValidationTransformer{
   161→			validateWalk: b.Operation == walkValidate,
   162→		},
   163→		&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},
   164→		&OutputTransformer{
   165→			Config:      b.Config,
   166→			RefreshOnly: b.skipPlanChanges || b.preDestroyRefresh,
   167→			Destroying:  b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,
   168→			Overrides:   b.Overrides,
   169→
   170→			// NOTE: We currently treat anything built with the plan graph
   171→			// builder as "planning" for our purposes here, because we share
   172→			// the same graph node implementation between all of the walk
   173→			// types and so the pre-planning walks still think they are
   174→			// producing a plan even though we immediately discard it.
   175→			Planning: true,
   176→		},
   177→
   178→		// Add nodes and edges for the check block assertions. Check block data
   179→		// sources were added earlier.
   180→		&checkTransformer{
   181→			Config:    b.Config,
   182→			Operation: b.Operation,
   183→		},
   184→
   185→		// Add orphan resources
   186→		&OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{
   187→			Concrete: b.ConcreteResourceOrphan,
   188→			State:    b.State,
   189→			Config:   b.Config,
   190→			skip:     b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,
   191→		},
   192→
   193→		// We also need nodes for any deposed instance objects present in the
   194→		// state, so we can plan to destroy them. (During plan this will
   195→		// intentionally skip creating nodes for _current_ objects, since
   196→		// ConfigTransformer created nodes that will do that during
   197→		// DynamicExpand.)
   198→		&StateTransformer{
   199→			ConcreteCurrent: b.ConcreteResourceInstance,
   200→			ConcreteDeposed: b.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed,

    18→// PlanGraphBuilder is a GraphBuilder implementation that builds a graph for
    19→// planning and for other "plan-like" operations which don't require an
    20→// already-calculated plan as input.
    21→//
    22→// Unlike the apply graph builder, this graph builder:
    23→//
    24→//   - Makes its decisions primarily based on the given configuration, which
    25→//     represents the desired state.
    26→//
    27→//   - Ignores certain lifecycle concerns like create_before_destroy, because
    28→//     those are only important once we already know what action we're planning
    29→//     to take against a particular resource instance.
    30→type PlanGraphBuilder struct {
    31→	// Config is the configuration tree to build a plan from.
    32→	Config *configs.Config
    33→
    34→	// State is the current state
    35→	State *states.State
    36→
    37→	// RootVariableValues are the raw input values for root input variables
    38→	// given by the caller, which we'll resolve into final values as part
    39→	// of the plan walk.
    40→	RootVariableValues InputValues
    41→
    42→	// ExternalProviderConfigs are pre-initialized root module provider
    43→	// configurations that the graph builder should assume will be available
    44→	// immediately during the subsequent plan walk, without any explicit
    45→	// initialization step.
    46→	ExternalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
    47→
    48→	// Plugins is a library of plug-in components (providers and
    49→	// provisioners) available for use.
    50→	Plugins *contextPlugins
    51→
    52→	// Targets are resources to target
    53→	Targets []addrs.Targetable
    54→
    55→	// ForceReplace are resource instances where if we would normally have
    56→	// generated a NoOp or Update action then we'll force generating a replace
    57→	// action instead. Create and Delete actions are not affected.
    58→	ForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    59→
    60→	// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing managed resources
    61→	skipRefresh bool
    62→
    63→	// preDestroyRefresh indicates that we are executing the refresh which
    64→	// happens immediately before a destroy plan, which happens to use the
    65→	// normal planing mode so skipPlanChanges cannot be set.
    66→	preDestroyRefresh bool
    67→
    68→	// skipPlanChanges indicates that we should skip the step of comparing
    69→	// prior state with configuration and generating planned changes to
    70→	// resource instances. (This is for the "refresh only" planning mode,
    71→	// where we _only_ do the refresh step.)
    72→	skipPlanChanges bool
    73→
    74→	ConcreteProvider                ConcreteProviderNodeFunc
    75→	ConcreteResource                ConcreteResourceNodeFunc
    76→	ConcreteResourceInstance        ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    77→	ConcreteResourceOrphan          ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc
    78→	ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc
    79→	ConcreteModule                  ConcreteModuleNodeFunc
    80→
    81→	// Plan Operation this graph will be used for.
    82→	Operation walkOperation
    83→
    84→	// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to
    85→	// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within
    86→	// the actual graph.
    87→	ExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference
    88→

    90→	// framework.
    91→	Overrides *mocking.Overrides
    92→
    93→	// ImportTargets are the list of resources to import.
    94→	ImportTargets []*ImportTarget
    95→
    96→	// forgetResources lists the resources that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed
    97→	// from state without destroying.
    98→	forgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource
    99→
   100→	// forgetModules lists the modules that are to be forgotten, i.e. removed
   101→	// from state without destroying.
   102→	forgetModules []addrs.Module
   103→
   104→	// GenerateConfig tells Terraform where to write and generated config for
   105→	// any import targets that do not already have configuration.
   106→	//
   107→	// If empty, then config will not be generated.
   108→	GenerateConfigPath string
   109→}
   110→
   111→// See GraphBuilder
   112→func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Build(path addrs.ModuleInstance) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   113→	log.Printf("[TRACE] building graph for %s", b.Operation)
   114→	return (&BasicGraphBuilder{
   115→		Steps: b.Steps(),
   116→		Name:  "PlanGraphBuilder",
   117→	}).Build(path)
   118→}
   119→
   120→// See GraphBuilder
   121→func (b *PlanGraphBuilder) Steps() []GraphTransformer {
   122→	switch b.Operation {
   123→	case walkPlan:
   124→		b.initPlan()
   125→	case walkPlanDestroy:
   126→		b.initDestroy()
   127→	case walkValidate:
   128→		b.initValidate()
   129→	case walkImport:
   130→		b.initImport()
   131→	default:
   132→		panic("invalid plan operation: " + b.Operation.String())
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	steps := []GraphTransformer{
   136→		// Creates all the resources represented in the config
   137→		&ConfigTransformer{
   138→			Concrete: b.ConcreteResource,
   139→			Config:   b.Config,
   140→			destroy:  b.Operation == walkDestroy || b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,
   141→
   142→			importTargets: b.ImportTargets,
   143→
   144→			// We only want to generate config during a plan operation.
   145→			generateConfigPathForImportTargets: b.GenerateConfigPath,
   146→		},
   147→
   148→		// Add dynamic values
   149→		&RootVariableTransformer{
   150→			Config:       b.Config,
   151→			RawValues:    b.RootVariableValues,
   152→			Planning:     true,
   153→			DestroyApply: false, // always false for planning
   154→		},
   155→		&ModuleVariableTransformer{
   156→			Config:       b.Config,
   157→			Planning:     true,
   158→			DestroyApply: false, // always false for planning
   159→		},
   160→		&variableValidationTransformer{
   161→			validateWalk: b.Operation == walkValidate,
   162→		},
   163→		&LocalTransformer{Config: b.Config},
   164→		&OutputTransformer{
   165→			Config:      b.Config,
   166→			RefreshOnly: b.skipPlanChanges || b.preDestroyRefresh,
   167→			Destroying:  b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,
   168→			Overrides:   b.Overrides,
   169→
   170→			// NOTE: We currently treat anything built with the plan graph
   171→			// builder as "planning" for our purposes here, because we share
   172→			// the same graph node implementation between all of the walk
   173→			// types and so the pre-planning walks still think they are
   174→			// producing a plan even though we immediately discard it.
   175→			Planning: true,
   176→		},
   177→
   178→		// Add nodes and edges for the check block assertions. Check block data
   179→		// sources were added earlier.
   180→		&checkTransformer{
   181→			Config:    b.Config,
   182→			Operation: b.Operation,
   183→		},
   184→
   185→		// Add orphan resources
   186→		&OrphanResourceInstanceTransformer{
   187→			Concrete: b.ConcreteResourceOrphan,
   188→			State:    b.State,
   189→			Config:   b.Config,
   190→			skip:     b.Operation == walkPlanDestroy,
   191→		},
   192→
   193→		// We also need nodes for any deposed instance objects present in the
   194→		// state, so we can plan to destroy them. (During plan this will
   195→		// intentionally skip creating nodes for _current_ objects, since
   196→		// ConfigTransformer created nodes that will do that during
   197→		// DynamicExpand.)
   198→		&StateTransformer{
   199→			ConcreteCurrent: b.ConcreteResourceInstance,
   200→			ConcreteDeposed: b.ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposed,

    71→	addr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()

    87→	config := n.Config

    92→	_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)

    95→		return diags
    96→	}

    98→	diags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))
    99→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   100→		return diags
   101→	}
   102→
   103→	checkRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error

   105→		checkRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning
   106→	}
   107→

   112→		return diags
   113→	}
   114→

   116→	// change at all. In which case even if we wanted to try and defer it
   117→	// (because of a dependency) we can't as there is no change to defer.
   118→	//
   119→	// The most common case for this is when the data source is being refreshed
   120→	// but depends on unknown values or dependencies which means we just skip
   121→	// refreshing the data source. We maintain that behaviour here.
   122→	if change != nil && deferred != nil {
   123→		// Then this data source got deferred by the provider during planning.
   124→		deferrals.ReportDataSourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)

   126→		// Not deferred; business as usual.
   127→
   128→		// write the data source into both the refresh state and the
   129→		// working state
   130→		diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, refreshState))
   131→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   132→			return diags
   133→		}
   134→		diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))
   135→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   136→			return diags
   137→		}

   139→		diags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, ""))

   142→		// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against
   143→		// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations
   144→		// until the user makes the condition succeed.

   155→}

   132→			return diags

   538→// replaceTriggered checks if this instance needs to be replace due to a change
   539→// in a replace_triggered_by reference. If replacement is required, the
   540→// instance address is added to forceReplace
   541→func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) replaceTriggered(ctx EvalContext, repData instances.RepetitionData) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   542→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   543→	if n.Config == nil {
   544→		return diags
   545→	}

   547→	for _, expr := range n.Config.TriggersReplacement {
   548→		ref, replace, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateReplaceTriggeredBy(expr, repData)
   549→		diags = diags.Append(evalDiags)
   550→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   551→			continue
   552→		}
   553→
   554→		if replace {
   555→			// FIXME: forceReplace accomplishes the same goal, however we may
   556→			// want to communicate more information about which resource
   557→			// triggered the replacement in the plan.
   558→			// Rather than further complicating the plan method with more
   559→			// options, we can refactor both of these features later.
   560→			n.forceReplace = append(n.forceReplace, n.Addr)
   561→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] ReplaceTriggeredBy forcing replacement of %s due to change in %s", n.Addr, ref.DisplayString())
   562→
   563→			n.replaceTriggeredBy = append(n.replaceTriggeredBy, ref)
   564→			break
   565→		}
   566→	}
   567→
   568→	return diags
   569→}

   319→		}
   320→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   321→			return diags
   322→		}
   323→	}
   324→
   325→	if n.skipRefresh && !importing && updatedCBD {
   326→		// CreateBeforeDestroy must be set correctly in the state which is used
   327→		// to create the apply graph, so if we did not refresh the state make
   328→		// sure we still update any changes to CreateBeforeDestroy.
   329→		diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))
   330→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   331→			return diags
   332→		}
   333→	}
   334→
   335→	// Plan the instance, unless we're in the refresh-only mode
   336→	if !n.skipPlanChanges {
   337→
   338→		// add this instance to n.forceReplace if replacement is triggered by
   339→		// another change
   340→		repData := instances.RepetitionData{}
   341→		switch k := addr.Resource.Key.(type) {
   342→		case addrs.IntKey:
   343→			repData.CountIndex = k.Value()
   344→		case addrs.StringKey:
   345→			repData.EachKey = k.Value()
   346→			repData.EachValue = cty.DynamicVal
   347→		}
   348→
   349→		diags = diags.Append(n.replaceTriggered(ctx, repData))
   350→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   351→			return diags
   352→		}
   353→
   354→		change, instancePlanState, planDeferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.plan(
   355→			ctx, nil, instanceRefreshState, n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy, n.forceReplace,
   356→		)
   357→		diags = diags.Append(planDiags)
   358→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   359→			// If we are importing and generating a configuration, we need to
   360→			// ensure the change is written out so the configuration can be
   361→			// captured.
   362→			if planDeferred == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {
   363→				// Update our return plan
   364→				change := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{
   365→					Addr:         n.Addr,
   366→					PrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),
   367→					ProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,
   368→					Change: plans.Change{
   369→						// we only need a placeholder, so this will be a NoOp
   370→						Action:          plans.NoOp,
   371→						Before:          instanceRefreshState.Value,
   372→						After:           instanceRefreshState.Value,
   373→						GeneratedConfig: n.generatedConfigHCL,
   374→					},
   375→				}
   376→				diags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, ""))
   377→			}
   378→
   379→			return diags
   380→		}
   381→
   382→		if deferred == nil && planDeferred != nil {
   383→			deferred = planDeferred
   384→		}
   385→
   386→		if importing {
   387→			change.Importing = &plans.Importing{ID: n.importTarget}
   388→		}
   389→
   390→		// FIXME: here we udpate the change to reflect the reason for
   391→		// replacement, but we still overload forceReplace to get the correct
   392→		// change planned.
   393→		if len(n.replaceTriggeredBy) > 0 {
   394→			change.ActionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceByTriggers
   395→		}
   396→
   397→		deferrals := ctx.Deferrals()
   398→		if deferred != nil {
   399→			// Then this resource has been deferred either during the import,
   400→			// refresh or planning stage. We'll report the deferral and
   401→			// store what we could produce in the deferral tracker.
   402→			deferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)
   403→		} else if !deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {
   404→			// We intentionally write the change before the subsequent checks, because
   405→			// all of the checks below this point are for problems caused by the
   406→			// context surrounding the change, rather than the change itself, and
   407→			// so it's helpful to still include the valid-in-isolation change as
   408→			// part of the plan as additional context in our error output.
   409→			//
   410→			// FIXME: it is currently important that we write resource changes to
   411→			// the plan (n.writeChange) before we write the corresponding state
   412→			// (n.writeResourceInstanceState).
   413→			//
   414→			// This is because the planned resource state will normally have the
   415→			// status of states.ObjectPlanned, which causes later logic to refer to
   416→			// the contents of the plan to retrieve the resource data. Because
   417→			// there is no shared lock between these two data structures, reversing
   418→			// the order of these writes will cause a brief window of inconsistency
   419→			// which can lead to a failed safety check.
   420→			//
   421→			// Future work should adjust these APIs such that it is impossible to
   422→			// update these two data structures incorrectly through any objects
   423→			// reachable via the terraform.EvalContext API.
   424→			diags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, ""))
   425→			if diags.HasErrors() {
   426→				return diags
   427→			}
   428→			diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instancePlanState, workingState))
   429→			if diags.HasErrors() {
   430→				return diags
   431→			}
   432→
   433→			diags = diags.Append(n.checkPreventDestroy(change))
   434→			if diags.HasErrors() {
   435→				return diags
   436→			}
   437→
   438→			// If this plan resulted in a NoOp, then apply won't have a chance to make
   439→			// any changes to the stored dependencies. Since this is a NoOp we know
   440→			// that the stored dependencies will have no effect during apply, and we can
   441→			// write them out now.
   442→			if change.Action == plans.NoOp && !depsEqual(instanceRefreshState.Dependencies, n.Dependencies) {
   443→				// the refresh state will be the final state for this resource, so
   444→				// finalize the dependencies here if they need to be updated.
   445→				instanceRefreshState.Dependencies = n.Dependencies
   446→				diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))
   447→				if diags.HasErrors() {
   448→					return diags
   449→				}
   450→			}
   451→
   452→			// Post-conditions might block completion. We intentionally do this
   453→			// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against
   454→			// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations
   455→			// until the user makes the condition succeed.
   456→			// (Note that some preconditions will end up being skipped during
   457→			// planning, because their conditions depend on values not yet known.)
   458→			checkDiags := evalCheckRules(
   459→				addrs.ResourcePostcondition,
   460→				n.Config.Postconditions,
   461→				ctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,
   462→				checkRuleSeverity,
   463→			)
   464→			diags = diags.Append(checkDiags)
   465→		} else {
   466→			// The deferrals tracker says that we must defer changes for
   467→			// this resource instance, presumably due to a dependency on an
   468→			// upstream object that was already deferred. Therefore we just
   469→			// report our own deferral (capturing a placeholder value in the
   470→			// deferral tracker) and don't add anything to the plan or
   471→			// working state.
   472→			// In this case, the expression evaluator should use the placeholder
   473→			// value registered here as the value of this resource instance,
   474→			// instead of using the plan.
   475→			deferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq, change)
   476→		}
   477→	} else {
   478→		// In refresh-only mode we need to evaluate the for-each expression in
   479→		// order to supply the value to the pre- and post-condition check
   480→		// blocks. This has the unfortunate edge case of a refresh-only plan
   481→		// executing with a for-each map which has the same keys but different
   482→		// values, which could result in a post-condition check relying on that
   483→		// value being inaccurate. Unless we decide to store the value of the
   484→		// for-each expression in state, this is unavoidable.
   485→		forEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)
   486→		repeatData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)
   487→
   488→		checkDiags := evalCheckRules(
   489→			addrs.ResourcePrecondition,
   490→			n.Config.Preconditions,
   491→			ctx, addr, repeatData,
   492→			checkRuleSeverity,
   493→		)
   494→		diags = diags.Append(checkDiags)
   495→
   496→		// Even if we don't plan changes, we do still need to at least update
   497→		// the working state to reflect the refresh result. If not, then e.g.
   498→		// any output values refering to this will not react to the drift.
   499→		// (Even if we didn't actually refresh above, this will still save
   500→		// the result of any schema upgrading we did in readResourceInstanceState.)
   501→		diags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, workingState))
   502→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   503→			return diags
   504→		}
   505→
   506→		// Here we also evaluate post-conditions after updating the working
   507→		// state, because we want to check against the result of the refresh.
   508→		// Unlike in normal planning mode, these checks are still evaluated
   509→		// even if pre-conditions generated diagnostics, because we have no
   510→		// planned changes to block.
   511→		checkDiags = evalCheckRules(
   512→			addrs.ResourcePostcondition,
   513→			n.Config.Postconditions,
   514→			ctx, addr, repeatData,
   515→			checkRuleSeverity,
   516→		)
   517→		diags = diags.Append(checkDiags)
   518→
   519→		// In this case we skipped planning changes and therefore need to report the deferral
   520→		// here, if there was one.
   521→		if refreshDeferred != nil {
   522→			ctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{
   523→				Addr:         n.Addr,
   524→				PrevRunAddr:  n.Addr,
   525→				ProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,
   526→				Change: plans.Change{
   527→					Action: plans.Read,
   528→					Before: priorInstanceRefreshState.Value,
   529→					After:  instanceRefreshState.Value,
   530→				},
   531→			})
   532→		}
   533→	}
   534→
   535→	return diags
   536→}
   537→
   538→// replaceTriggered checks if this instance needs to be replace due to a change
   539→// in a replace_triggered_by reference. If replacement is required, the
   540→// instance address is added to forceReplace
   541→func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) replaceTriggered(ctx EvalContext, repData instances.RepetitionData) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   542→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   543→	if n.Config == nil {
   544→		return diags
   545→	}
   546→
   547→	for _, expr := range n.Config.TriggersReplacement {
   548→		ref, replace, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateReplaceTriggeredBy(expr, repData)
   549→		diags = diags.Append(evalDiags)
   550→		if diags.HasErrors() {
   551→			continue
   552→		}
   553→
   554→		if replace {
   555→			// FIXME: forceReplace accomplishes the same goal, however we may
   556→			// want to communicate more information about which resource
   557→			// triggered the replacement in the plan.
   558→			// Rather than further complicating the plan method with more
   559→			// options, we can refactor both of these features later.
   560→			n.forceReplace = append(n.forceReplace, n.Addr)
   561→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] ReplaceTriggeredBy forcing replacement of %s due to change in %s", n.Addr, ref.DisplayString())
   562→
   563→			n.replaceTriggeredBy = append(n.replaceTriggeredBy, ref)
   564→			break
   565→		}
   566→	}
   567→
   568→	return diags
   569→}
   570→
   571→func (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) importState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, importId string, provider providers.Interface, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {

743→
   744→func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) plan(
   745→	ctx EvalContext,
   746→	plannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,
   747→	currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject,
   748→	createBeforeDestroy bool,
   749→	forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance,
   750→) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   751→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   752→	var keyData instances.RepetitionData
   753→	var deferred *providers.Deferred
   754→
   755→	resource := n.Addr.Resource.Resource
   756→	deferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()
   757→
   758→	provider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)
   759→	if err != nil {
   760→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags.Append(err)
   761→	}
   762→
   763→	schema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(resource)
   764→	if schema == nil {
   765→		// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here
   766→		diags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf("provider does not support resource type %q", resource.Type))
   767→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags
   768→	}
   769→
   770→	// If we're importing and generating config, generate it now.
   771→	if n.Config == nil {
   772→		// This shouldn't happen. A node that isn't generating config should
   773→		// have embedded config, and the rest of Terraform should enforce this.
   774→		// If, however, we didn't do things correctly the next line will panic,
   775→		// so let's not do that and return an error message with more context.
   776→
   777→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   778→			tfdiags.Error,
   779→			"Resource has no configuration",
   780→			fmt.Sprintf("Terraform attempted to process a resource at %s that has no configuration. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!", n.Addr.String())))
   781→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags
   782→	}
   783→
   784→	config := *n.Config
   785→
   786→	checkRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error
   787→	if n.preDestroyRefresh {
   788→		checkRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning
   789→	}
   790→
   791→	if plannedChange != nil {
   792→		// If we already planned the action, we stick to that plan
   793→		createBeforeDestroy = plannedChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete
   794→	}
   795→
   796→	// Evaluate the configuration
   797→	forEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)
   798→
   799→	keyData = EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)
   800→
   801→	checkDiags := evalCheckRules(
   802→		addrs.ResourcePrecondition,
   803→		n.Config.Preconditions,
   804→		ctx, n.Addr, keyData,
   805→		checkRuleSeverity,
   806→	)
   807→	diags = diags.Append(checkDiags)
   808→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   809→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags // failed preconditions prevent further evaluation
   810→	}
   811→
   812→	// If we have a previous plan and the action was a noop, then the only
   813→	// reason we're in this method was to evaluate the preconditions. There's
   814→	// no need to re-plan this resource.
   815→	if plannedChange != nil && plannedChange.Action == plans.NoOp {
   816→		return plannedChange, currentState.DeepCopy(), deferred, keyData, diags
   817→	}
   818→
   819→	origConfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)
   820→	diags = diags.Append(configDiags)
   821→	diags = diags.Append(
   822→		validateResourceForbiddenEphemeralValues(ctx, origConfigVal, schema).InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String()),
   823→	)
   824→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   825→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags
   826→	}
   827→
   828→	metaConfigVal, metaDiags := n.providerMetas(ctx)
   829→	diags = diags.Append(metaDiags)
   830→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   831→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags
   832→	}
   833→
   834→	var priorVal cty.Value
   835→	var priorValTainted cty.Value
   836→	var priorPrivate []byte
   837→	if currentState != nil {
   838→		if currentState.Status != states.ObjectTainted {
   839→			priorVal = currentState.Value
   840→			priorPrivate = currentState.Private
   841→		} else {
   842→			// If the prior state is tainted then we'll proceed below like
   843→			// we're creating an entirely new object, but then turn it into
   844→			// a synthetic "Replace" change at the end, creating the same
   845→			// result as if the provider had marked at least one argument
   846→			// change as "requires replacement".
   847→			priorValTainted = currentState.Value
   848→			priorVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())
   849→		}
   850→	} else {
   851→		priorVal = cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType())
   852→	}
   853→
   854→	log.Printf("[TRACE] Re-validating config for %q", n.Addr)
   855→	// Allow the provider to validate the final set of values.  The config was
   856→	// statically validated early on, but there may have been unknown values
   857→	// which the provider could not validate at the time.
   858→	//
   859→	// TODO: It would be more correct to validate the config after
   860→	// ignore_changes has been applied, but the current implementation cannot
   861→	// exclude computed-only attributes when given the `all` option.
   862→
   863→	// we must unmark and use the original config, since the ignore_changes
   864→	// handling below needs access to the marks.
   865→	unmarkedConfigVal, _ := origConfigVal.UnmarkDeep()
   866→	validateResp := provider.ValidateResourceConfig(
   867→		providers.ValidateResourceConfigRequest{
   868→			TypeName: n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,
   869→			Config:   unmarkedConfigVal,
   870→		},
   871→	)
   872→	diags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))
   873→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   874→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags
   875→	}
   876→
   877→	// ignore_changes is meant to only apply to the configuration, so it must
   878→	// be applied before we generate a plan. This ensures the config used for
   879→	// the proposed value, the proposed value itself, and the config presented
   880→	// to the provider in the PlanResourceChange request all agree on the
   881→	// starting values.
   882→	// Here we operate on the marked values, so as to revert any changes to the
   883→	// marks as well as the value.
   884→	configValIgnored, ignoreChangeDiags := n.processIgnoreChanges(priorVal, origConfigVal, schema)
   885→	diags = diags.Append(ignoreChangeDiags)
   886→	if ignoreChangeDiags.HasErrors() {
   887→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags
   888→	}
   889→
   890→	// Create an unmarked version of our config val and our prior val.
   891→	// Store the paths for the config val to re-mark after we've sent things
   892→	// over the wire.
   893→	unmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configValIgnored.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()
   894→	unmarkedPriorVal, _ := priorVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()
   895→
   896→	proposedNewVal := objchange.ProposedNew(schema, unmarkedPriorVal, unmarkedConfigVal)
   897→
   898→	// Call pre-diff hook
   899→	diags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {
   900→		return h.PreDiff(n.HookResourceIdentity(), addrs.NotDeposed, priorVal, proposedNewVal)
   901→	}))
   902→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   903→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags
   904→	}
   905→
   906→	var resp providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse
   907→	if n.override != nil {
   908→		// Then we have an override to apply for this change. But, overrides
   909→		// only matter when we are creating a resource for the first time as we
   910→		// only apply computed values.
   911→		if priorVal.IsNull() {
   912→			// Then we are actually creating something, so let's populate the
   913→			// computed values from our override value.
   914→			override, overrideDiags := mocking.PlanComputedValuesForResource(proposedNewVal, schema)
   915→			resp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{
   916→				PlannedState: override,
   917→				Diagnostics:  overrideDiags,
   918→			}
   919→		} else {
   920→			// This is an update operation, and we don't actually have any
   921→			// computed values that need to be applied.
   922→			resp = providers.PlanResourceChangeResponse{
   923→				PlannedState: proposedNewVal,
   924→			}
   925→		}
   926→	} else {
   927→		resp = provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{
   928→			TypeName:         n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type,
   929→			Config:           unmarkedConfigVal,
   930→			PriorState:       unmarkedPriorVal,
   931→			ProposedNewState: proposedNewVal,
   932→			PriorPrivate:     priorPrivate,
   933→			ProviderMeta:     metaConfigVal,
   934→			ClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{
   935→				DeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,
   936→			},
   937→		})
   938→		// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not
   939→		// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.
   940→		if resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {
   941→			diags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))
   942→		}
   943→	}
   944→	diags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(config.Config, n.Addr.String()))
   945→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   946→		return nil, nil, deferred, keyData, diags
   947→	}
   948→
   949→	// We mark this node as deferred at a later point when we know the complete change
   950→	if resp.Deferred != nil {
   951→		deferred = resp.Deferred

579→// if the second return value is non-nil, the refresh is deferred
   580→func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) refresh(ctx EvalContext, deposedKey states.DeposedKey, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, deferralAllowed bool) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   581→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   582→	var deferred *providers.Deferred
   583→	absAddr := n.Addr
   584→	if deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {
   585→		log.Printf("[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.refresh for %s", absAddr)
   586→	} else {
   587→		log.Printf("[TRACE] NodeAbstractResourceInstance.refresh for %s (deposed object %s)", absAddr, deposedKey)
   588→	}
   589→	provider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)
   590→	if err != nil {
   591→		return state, deferred, diags.Append(err)
   592→	}
   593→	// If we have no state, we don't do any refreshing

   595→		log.Printf("[DEBUG] refresh: %s: no state, so not refreshing", absAddr)

   598→
   599→	schema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.Resource.ContainingResource())

   601→		// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here
   602→		diags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf("provider does not support resource type %q", n.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type))

   607→	diags = diags.Append(metaDiags)

   611→

   614→		return h.PreRefresh(n.HookResourceIdentity(), deposedKey, state.Value)
   615→	}))
   616→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   617→		return state, deferred, diags
   618→	}
   619→
   620→	// Refresh!
   621→	priorVal := state.Value
   622→
   623→	// Unmarked before sending to provider
   624→	var priorMarks []cty.PathValueMarks

   641→				DeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,

   649→		}

   651→		if resp.Deferred != nil {
   652→			deferred = resp.Deferred
   653→		}
   654→	}
   655→	if n.Config != nil {
   656→		resp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String())
   657→	}
   658→
   659→	diags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)

   661→		return state, deferred, diags
   662→	}
   663→

   665→		// This ought not to happen in real cases since it's not possible to

   722→

   637→			PriorState:   priorVal,
   638→			Private:      state.Private,
   639→			ProviderMeta: metaConfigVal,
   640→			ClientCapabilities: providers.ClientCapabilities{
   641→				DeferralAllowed: deferralAllowed,
   642→			},
   643→		})
   644→
   645→		// If we don't support deferrals, but the provider reports a deferral and does not
   646→		// emit any error level diagnostics, we should emit an error.
   647→		if resp.Deferred != nil && !deferralAllowed && !resp.Diagnostics.HasErrors() {
   648→			diags = diags.Append(deferring.UnexpectedProviderDeferralDiagnostic(n.Addr))
   649→		}
   650→
   651→		if resp.Deferred != nil {
   652→			deferred = resp.Deferred
   653→		}
   654→	}
   655→	if n.Config != nil {
   656→		resp.Diagnostics = resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.Config.Config, n.Addr.String())
   657→	}
   658→
   659→	diags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics)
   660→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   661→		return state, deferred, diags
   662→	}
   663→
   664→	if resp.NewState == cty.NilVal {
   665→		// This ought not to happen in real cases since it's not possible to
   666→		// send NilVal over the plugin RPC channel, but it can come up in
   667→		// tests due to sloppy mocking.
   668→		panic("new state is cty.NilVal")
   669→	}
   670→
   671→	// If we have deferred the refresh, we expect the new state not to be wholly known
   672→	// and callers should be prepared to handle this.
   673→	if !resp.NewState.IsWhollyKnown() && deferred == nil {
   674→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   675→			tfdiags.Error,
   676→			"Provider produced invalid object",
   677→			fmt.Sprintf(
   678→				"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s during refresh: %s.\n\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.",
   679→				n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, absAddr, "The returned state contains unknown values",
   680→			),
   681→		))
   682→	}
   683→
   684→	for _, err := range resp.NewState.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {
   685→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   686→			tfdiags.Error,
   687→			"Provider produced invalid object",
   688→			fmt.Sprintf(
   689→				"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s during refresh: %s.\n\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.",
   690→				n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, absAddr, tfdiags.FormatError(err),
   691→			),
   692→		))
   693→	}
   694→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   695→		return state, deferred, diags
   696→	}
   697→
   698→	newState := objchange.NormalizeObjectFromLegacySDK(resp.NewState, schema)
   699→	if !newState.RawEquals(resp.NewState) {
   700→		// We had to fix up this object in some way, and we still need to
   701→		// accept any changes for compatibility, so all we can do is log a
   702→		// warning about the change.
   703→		log.Printf("[WARN] Provider %q produced an invalid new value containing null blocks for %q during refresh\n", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider, n.Addr)
   704→	}
   705→
   706→	ret := state.DeepCopy()
   707→	ret.Value = newState
   708→	ret.Private = resp.Private
   709→
   710→	// We have no way to exempt provider using the legacy SDK from this check,
   711→	// so we can only log inconsistencies with the updated state values.
   712→	// In most cases these are not errors anyway, and represent "drift" from
   713→	// external changes which will be handled by the subsequent plan.
   714→	if errs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, priorVal, ret.Value); len(errs) > 0 {
   715→		var buf strings.Builder
   716→		fmt.Fprintf(&buf, "[WARN] Provider %q produced an unexpected new value for %s during refresh.", n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), absAddr)
   717→		for _, err := range errs {
   718→			fmt.Fprintf(&buf, "\n      - %s", tfdiags.FormatError(err))
   719→		}
   720→		log.Print(buf.String())
   721→	}
   722→
   723→	// Call post-refresh hook
   724→	diags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {
   725→		return h.PostRefresh(n.HookResourceIdentity(), deposedKey, priorVal, ret.Value)
   726→	}))
   727→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   728→		return ret, deferred, diags
   729→	}
   730→
   731→	// Mark the value with any prior marks from the state, and the marks from
   732→	// the schema. This ensures we capture any marks from the last
   733→	// configuration, as well as any marks from the schema which were not in
   734→	// the prior state. New marks may appear when the prior state was from an
   735→	// import operation, or if the provider added new marks to the schema.
   736→	ret.Value = ret.Value.MarkWithPaths(priorMarks)
   737→	if moreSensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(ret.Value, nil); len(moreSensitivePaths) != 0 {
   738→		ret.Value = marks.MarkPaths(ret.Value, marks.Sensitive, moreSensitivePaths)
   739→	}
   740→
   741→	return ret, deferred, diags
   742→}
   743→
   744→func (n *NodeAbstractResourceInstance) plan(
   745→	ctx EvalContext,
   746→	plannedChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange,
   747→	currentState *states.ResourceInstanceObject,
   748→	createBeforeDestroy bool,
   749→	forceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance,
   750→) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, *states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, instances.RepetitionData, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   751→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   752→	var keyData instances.RepetitionData

   754→
   755→	resource := n.Addr.Resource.Resource

   766→		diags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf("provider does not support resource type %q", resource.Type))

   774→		// If, however, we didn't do things correctly the next line will panic,

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→	"sort"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    16→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/langrefs"
    17→)
    18→
    19→// GraphNodeReferenceable must be implemented by any node that represents
    20→// a Terraform thing that can be referenced (resource, module, etc.).
    21→//
    22→// Even if the thing has no name, this should return an empty list. By
    23→// implementing this and returning a non-nil result, you say that this CAN
    24→// be referenced and other methods of referencing may still be possible (such
    25→// as by path!)
    26→type GraphNodeReferenceable interface {
    27→	GraphNodeModulePath
    28→
    29→	// ReferenceableAddrs returns a list of addresses through which this can be
    30→	// referenced.
    31→	ReferenceableAddrs() []addrs.Referenceable
    32→}
    33→
    34→// GraphNodeReferencer must be implemented by nodes that reference other
    35→// Terraform items and therefore depend on them.
    36→type GraphNodeReferencer interface {
    37→	GraphNodeModulePath
    38→
    39→	// References returns a list of references made by this node, which
    40→	// include both a referenced address and source location information for
    41→	// the reference.
    42→	References() []*addrs.Reference
    43→}
    44→
    45→// GraphNodeReferencer must be implemented by nodes that import resources.
    46→type GraphNodeImportReferencer interface {
    47→	GraphNodeReferencer
    48→
    49→	// ImportReferences returns a list of references made by this node's
    50→	// associated import block.
    51→	ImportReferences() []*addrs.Reference
    52→}
    53→
    54→type GraphNodeAttachDependencies interface {
    55→	AttachDependencies([]addrs.ConfigResource)
    56→}
    57→
    58→// graphNodeDependsOn is implemented by resources that need to expose any
    59→// references set via DependsOn in their configuration.
    60→type graphNodeDependsOn interface {
    61→	GraphNodeReferencer
    62→	DependsOn() []*addrs.Reference
    63→}
    64→
    65→// graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn records all resources that are transitively
    66→// referenced through depends_on in the configuration. This is used by data
    67→// resources to determine if they can be read during the plan, or if they need
    68→// to be further delayed until apply.
    69→// We can only use an addrs.ConfigResource address here, because modules are
    70→// not yet expended in the graph. While this will cause some extra data
    71→// resources to show in the plan when their depends_on references may be in
    72→// unrelated module instances, the fact that it only happens when there are any
    73→// resource updates pending means we can still avoid the problem of the
    74→// "perpetual diff"
    75→type graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn interface {
    76→	GraphNodeConfigResource
    77→	graphNodeDependsOn
    78→
    79→	// AttachDataResourceDependsOn stores the discovered dependencies in the
    80→	// resource node for evaluation later.
    81→	AttachDataResourceDependsOn(deps []addrs.ConfigResource)
    82→}
    83→
    84→// GraphNodeReferenceOutside is an interface that can optionally be implemented.
    85→// A node that implements it can specify that its own referenceable addresses
    86→// and/or the addresses it references are in a different module than the
    87→// node itself.
    88→//
    89→// Any referenceable addresses returned by ReferenceableAddrs are interpreted
    90→// relative to the returned selfPath.
    91→//
    92→// Any references returned by References are interpreted relative to the
    93→// returned referencePath.
    94→//
    95→// It is valid but not required for either of these paths to match what is
    96→// returned by method Path, though if both match the main Path then there
    97→// is no reason to implement this method.
    98→//
    99→// The primary use-case for this is the nodes representing module input
   100→// variables, since their expressions are resolved in terms of their calling
   101→// module, but they are still referenced from their own module.
   102→type GraphNodeReferenceOutside interface {
   103→	// ReferenceOutside returns a path in which any references from this node
   104→	// are resolved.
   105→	ReferenceOutside() (selfPath, referencePath addrs.Module)
   106→}
   107→
   108→// ReferenceTransformer is a GraphTransformer that connects all the
   109→// nodes that reference each other in order to form the proper ordering.
   110→type ReferenceTransformer struct{}
   111→
   112→func (t *ReferenceTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   113→	// Build a reference map so we can efficiently look up the references
   114→	vs := g.Vertices()
   115→	m := NewReferenceMap(vs)
   116→
   117→	// Find the things that reference things and connect them
   118→	for _, v := range vs {
   119→		if _, ok := v.(GraphNodeDestroyer); ok {
   120→			// destroy nodes references are not connected, since they can only
   121→			// use their own state.
   122→			continue
   123→		}
   124→
   125→		parents := m.References(v)
   126→		parentsDbg := make([]string, len(parents))
   127→		for i, v := range parents {
   128→			parentsDbg[i] = dag.VertexName(v)
   129→		}
   130→		log.Printf(
   131→			"[DEBUG] ReferenceTransformer: %q references: %v",
   132→			dag.VertexName(v), parentsDbg)
   133→
   134→		for _, parent := range parents {
   135→			// A destroy plan relies solely on the state, so we only need to
   136→			// ensure that temporary values are connected to get the evaluation
   137→			// order correct. Any references to destroy nodes will cause
   138→			// cycles, because they are connected in reverse order.
   139→			if _, ok := parent.(GraphNodeDestroyer); ok {
   140→				continue
   141→			}
   142→
   143→			if !graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule(v, parent) {
   144→				g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(v, parent))
   145→			} else {
   146→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ReferenceTransformer: skipping %s => %s inter-module-instance dependency", dag.VertexName(v), dag.VertexName(parent))
   147→			}
   148→		}
   149→
   150→		if len(parents) > 0 {
   151→			continue
   152→		}
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	return nil
   156→}
   157→
   158→type depMap map[string]addrs.ConfigResource
   159→
   160→// add stores the vertex if it represents a resource in the
   161→// graph.
   162→func (m depMap) add(v dag.Vertex) {
   163→	// we're only concerned with resources which may have changes that
   164→	// need to be applied.
   165→	switch v := v.(type) {
   166→	case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   167→		instAddr := v.ResourceInstanceAddr()
   168→		addr := instAddr.ContainingResource().Config()
   169→		m[addr.String()] = addr
   170→	case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   171→		addr := v.ResourceAddr()
   172→		m[addr.String()] = addr
   173→	}
   174→}
   175→
   176→// attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer records all resources transitively
   177→// referenced through a configuration depends_on.
   178→type attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer struct {
   179→}
   180→
   181→func (t attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   182→	// First we need to make a map of referenceable addresses to their vertices.
   183→	// This is very similar to what's done in ReferenceTransformer, but we keep
   184→	// implementation separate as they may need to change independently.
   185→	vertices := g.Vertices()
   186→	refMap := NewReferenceMap(vertices)
   187→
   188→	for _, v := range vertices {
   189→		depender, ok := v.(graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn)
   190→		if !ok {
   191→			continue
   192→		}
   193→
   194→		// Only data need to attach depends_on, so they can determine if they
   195→		// are eligible to be read during plan.
   196→		if depender.ResourceAddr().Resource.Mode != addrs.DataResourceMode {
   197→			continue
   198→		}
   199→
   200→		// depMap will only add resource references then dedupe
   201→		deps := make(depMap)
   202→		dependsOnDeps := refMap.dependsOn(g, depender)
   203→		for _, dep := range dependsOnDeps {
   204→			// any the dependency
   205→			deps.add(dep)
   206→		}
   207→
   208→		res := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(deps))
   209→		for _, d := range deps {
   210→			res = append(res, d)
   211→		}
   212→
   213→		log.Printf("[TRACE] attachDataDependenciesTransformer: %s depends on %s", depender.ResourceAddr(), res)
   214→		depender.AttachDataResourceDependsOn(res)
   215→	}
   216→
   217→	return nil
   218→}
   219→
   220→// AttachDependenciesTransformer records all resource dependencies for each
   221→// instance, and attaches the addresses to the node itself. Managed resource
   222→// will record these in the state for proper ordering of destroy operations.
   223→type AttachDependenciesTransformer struct {
   224→}
   225→
   226→func (t AttachDependenciesTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   227→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
   228→		attacher, ok := v.(GraphNodeAttachDependencies)
   229→		if !ok {
   230→			continue
   231→		}
   232→
   233→		// We'll check if the node is a config resource already, in which case
   234→		// we want to make sure it is not referencing itself.
   235→
   236→		// matchesSelf is a function that returns true if the given address
   237→		// matches the address of the node itself.
   238→		matchesSelf := func(addrs.ConfigResource) bool {
   239→			// Default case is to always return false.
   240→			return false
   241→		}
   242→
   243→		self, ok := v.(GraphNodeConfigResource)
   244→		if ok {
   245→			matchesSelf = func(addr addrs.ConfigResource) bool {
   246→				// If we know the node is a config resource, we can compare
   247→				// the addresses directly.
   248→				return addr.Equal(self.ResourceAddr())
   249→			}
   250→		}
   251→
   252→		// dedupe addrs when there's multiple instances involved, or
   253→		// multiple paths in the un-reduced graph
   254→		depMap := map[string]addrs.ConfigResource{}
   255→
   256→		// since we need to type-switch over the nodes anyway, we're going to
   257→		// insert the address directly into depMap and forget about the returned
   258→		// set.
   259→		for _, d := range g.Ancestors(v) {
   260→			var addr addrs.ConfigResource
   261→
   262→			switch d := d.(type) {
   263→			case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   264→				instAddr := d.ResourceInstanceAddr()
   265→				addr = instAddr.ContainingResource().Config()
   266→			case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   267→				addr = d.ResourceAddr()
   268→			default:
   269→				continue
   270→			}
   271→
   272→			if matchesSelf(addr) {
   273→				continue
   274→			}
   275→
   276→			depMap[addr.String()] = addr
   277→		}
   278→
   279→		deps := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(depMap))
   280→		for _, d := range depMap {
   281→			deps = append(deps, d)
   282→		}
   283→		sort.Slice(deps, func(i, j int) bool {
   284→			return deps[i].String() < deps[j].String()
   285→		})
   286→
   287→		log.Printf("[TRACE] AttachDependenciesTransformer: %s depends on %s", dag.VertexName(v), deps)
   288→		attacher.AttachDependencies(deps)
   289→	}
   290→
   291→	return nil
   292→}
   293→
   294→func isDependableResource(v dag.Vertex) bool {
   295→	switch v.(type) {
   296→	case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   297→		return true
   298→	case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   299→		return true
   300→	}

    19→// GraphNodeReferenceable must be implemented by any node that represents
    20→// a Terraform thing that can be referenced (resource, module, etc.).
    21→//
    22→// Even if the thing has no name, this should return an empty list. By
    23→// implementing this and returning a non-nil result, you say that this CAN

    32→}

    38→
    39→	// References returns a list of references made by this node, which
    40→	// include both a referenced address and source location information for
    41→	// the reference.

    68→// to be further delayed until apply.

   108→// ReferenceTransformer is a GraphTransformer that connects all the
   109→// nodes that reference each other in order to form the proper ordering.
   110→type ReferenceTransformer struct{}
   111→
   112→func (t *ReferenceTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   113→	// Build a reference map so we can efficiently look up the references
   114→	vs := g.Vertices()
   115→	m := NewReferenceMap(vs)
   116→
   117→	// Find the things that reference things and connect them
   118→	for _, v := range vs {
   119→		if _, ok := v.(GraphNodeDestroyer); ok {
   120→			// destroy nodes references are not connected, since they can only
   121→			// use their own state.
   122→			continue
   123→		}
   124→
   125→		parents := m.References(v)
   126→		parentsDbg := make([]string, len(parents))
   127→		for i, v := range parents {
   128→			parentsDbg[i] = dag.VertexName(v)
   129→		}
   130→		log.Printf(
   131→			"[DEBUG] ReferenceTransformer: %q references: %v",
   132→			dag.VertexName(v), parentsDbg)
   133→
   134→		for _, parent := range parents {
   135→			// A destroy plan relies solely on the state, so we only need to
   136→			// ensure that temporary values are connected to get the evaluation
   137→			// order correct. Any references to destroy nodes will cause
   138→			// cycles, because they are connected in reverse order.
   139→			if _, ok := parent.(GraphNodeDestroyer); ok {
   140→				continue
   141→			}
   142→
   143→			if !graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule(v, parent) {
   144→				g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(v, parent))
   145→			} else {
   146→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ReferenceTransformer: skipping %s => %s inter-module-instance dependency", dag.VertexName(v), dag.VertexName(parent))
   147→			}
   148→		}
   149→
   150→		if len(parents) > 0 {
   151→			continue
   152→		}
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	return nil
   156→}

   158→type depMap map[string]addrs.ConfigResource
   159→
   160→// add stores the vertex if it represents a resource in the
   161→// graph.
   162→func (m depMap) add(v dag.Vertex) {
   163→	// we're only concerned with resources which may have changes that
   164→	// need to be applied.
   165→	switch v := v.(type) {
   166→	case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   167→		instAddr := v.ResourceInstanceAddr()
   168→		addr := instAddr.ContainingResource().Config()
   169→		m[addr.String()] = addr
   170→	case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   171→		addr := v.ResourceAddr()
   172→		m[addr.String()] = addr
   173→	}
   174→}

   176→// attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer records all resources transitively
   177→// referenced through a configuration depends_on.
   178→type attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer struct {
   179→}
   180→
   181→func (t attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   182→	// First we need to make a map of referenceable addresses to their vertices.

   192→		}
