   198→		}
   199→
   200→		// depMap will only add resource references then dedupe
   201→		deps := make(depMap)
   202→		dependsOnDeps := refMap.dependsOn(g, depender)
   203→		for _, dep := range dependsOnDeps {
   204→			// any the dependency
   205→			deps.add(dep)
   206→		}
   207→
   208→		res := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(deps))
   209→		for _, d := range deps {
   210→			res = append(res, d)
   211→		}
   212→
   213→		log.Printf("[TRACE] attachDataDependenciesTransformer: %s depends on %s", depender.ResourceAddr(), res)
   214→		depender.AttachDataResourceDependsOn(res)
   215→	}
   216→
   217→	return nil
   218→}
   219→
   220→// AttachDependenciesTransformer records all resource dependencies for each
   221→// instance, and attaches the addresses to the node itself. Managed resource
   222→// will record these in the state for proper ordering of destroy operations.
   223→type AttachDependenciesTransformer struct {
   224→}
   225→
   226→func (t AttachDependenciesTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   227→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
   228→		attacher, ok := v.(GraphNodeAttachDependencies)
   229→		if !ok {
   230→			continue
   231→		}
   232→
   233→		// We'll check if the node is a config resource already, in which case
   234→		// we want to make sure it is not referencing itself.
   235→
   236→		// matchesSelf is a function that returns true if the given address
   237→		// matches the address of the node itself.
   238→		matchesSelf := func(addrs.ConfigResource) bool {
   239→			// Default case is to always return false.
   240→			return false
   241→		}
   242→
   243→		self, ok := v.(GraphNodeConfigResource)
   244→		if ok {
   245→			matchesSelf = func(addr addrs.ConfigResource) bool {
   246→				// If we know the node is a config resource, we can compare
   247→				// the addresses directly.
   248→				return addr.Equal(self.ResourceAddr())
   249→			}
   250→		}
   251→
   252→		// dedupe addrs when there's multiple instances involved, or
   253→		// multiple paths in the un-reduced graph
   254→		depMap := map[string]addrs.ConfigResource{}
   255→
   256→		// since we need to type-switch over the nodes anyway, we're going to
   257→		// insert the address directly into depMap and forget about the returned
   258→		// set.
   259→		for _, d := range g.Ancestors(v) {
   260→			var addr addrs.ConfigResource
   261→

   263→			case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   264→				instAddr := d.ResourceInstanceAddr()
   265→				addr = instAddr.ContainingResource().Config()
   266→			case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   267→				addr = d.ResourceAddr()

   269→				continue
   270→			}
   271→
   272→			if matchesSelf(addr) {
   273→				continue
   274→			}
   275→
   276→			depMap[addr.String()] = addr
   277→		}
   278→
   279→		deps := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(depMap))
   280→		for _, d := range depMap {
   281→			deps = append(deps, d)
   282→		}
   283→		sort.Slice(deps, func(i, j int) bool {
   284→			return deps[i].String() < deps[j].String()
   285→		})
   286→
   287→		log.Printf("[TRACE] AttachDependenciesTransformer: %s depends on %s", dag.VertexName(v), deps)
   288→		attacher.AttachDependencies(deps)
   289→	}
   290→
   291→	return nil
   292→}
   293→
   294→func isDependableResource(v dag.Vertex) bool {
   295→	switch v.(type) {
   296→	case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   297→		return true
   298→	case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   299→		return true
   300→	}

   142→
   143→			if !graphNodesAreResourceInstancesInDifferentInstancesOfSameModule(v, parent) {
   144→				g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(v, parent))
   145→			} else {
   146→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ReferenceTransformer: skipping %s => %s inter-module-instance dependency", dag.VertexName(v), dag.VertexName(parent))
   147→			}
   148→		}
   149→
   150→		if len(parents) > 0 {
   151→			continue
   152→		}
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	return nil
   156→}
   157→
   158→type depMap map[string]addrs.ConfigResource
   159→
   160→// add stores the vertex if it represents a resource in the
   161→// graph.
   162→func (m depMap) add(v dag.Vertex) {
   163→	// we're only concerned with resources which may have changes that
   164→	// need to be applied.
   165→	switch v := v.(type) {
   166→	case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   167→		instAddr := v.ResourceInstanceAddr()
   168→		addr := instAddr.ContainingResource().Config()
   169→		m[addr.String()] = addr
   170→	case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   171→		addr := v.ResourceAddr()
   172→		m[addr.String()] = addr
   173→	}
   174→}
   175→
   176→// attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer records all resources transitively
   177→// referenced through a configuration depends_on.
   178→type attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer struct {
   179→}
   180→
   181→func (t attachDataResourceDependsOnTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   182→	// First we need to make a map of referenceable addresses to their vertices.
   183→	// This is very similar to what's done in ReferenceTransformer, but we keep
   184→	// implementation separate as they may need to change independently.
   185→	vertices := g.Vertices()
   186→	refMap := NewReferenceMap(vertices)
   187→
   188→	for _, v := range vertices {
   189→		depender, ok := v.(graphNodeAttachDataResourceDependsOn)
   190→		if !ok {
   191→			continue
   192→		}
   193→
   194→		// Only data need to attach depends_on, so they can determine if they
   195→		// are eligible to be read during plan.
   196→		if depender.ResourceAddr().Resource.Mode != addrs.DataResourceMode {
   197→			continue
   198→		}
   199→
   200→		// depMap will only add resource references then dedupe
   201→		deps := make(depMap)
   202→		dependsOnDeps := refMap.dependsOn(g, depender)
   203→		for _, dep := range dependsOnDeps {
   204→			// any the dependency
   205→			deps.add(dep)
   206→		}
   207→
   208→		res := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(deps))
   209→		for _, d := range deps {
   210→			res = append(res, d)
   211→		}
   212→
   213→		log.Printf("[TRACE] attachDataDependenciesTransformer: %s depends on %s", depender.ResourceAddr(), res)
   214→		depender.AttachDataResourceDependsOn(res)
   215→	}
   216→
   217→	return nil
   218→}
   219→
   220→// AttachDependenciesTransformer records all resource dependencies for each
   221→// instance, and attaches the addresses to the node itself. Managed resource
   222→// will record these in the state for proper ordering of destroy operations.
   223→type AttachDependenciesTransformer struct {
   224→}
   225→
   226→func (t AttachDependenciesTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   227→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
   228→		attacher, ok := v.(GraphNodeAttachDependencies)
   229→		if !ok {
   230→			continue
   231→		}
   232→
   233→		// We'll check if the node is a config resource already, in which case
   234→		// we want to make sure it is not referencing itself.
   235→
   236→		// matchesSelf is a function that returns true if the given address
   237→		// matches the address of the node itself.
   238→		matchesSelf := func(addrs.ConfigResource) bool {
   239→			// Default case is to always return false.
   240→			return false
   241→		}
   242→
   243→		self, ok := v.(GraphNodeConfigResource)
   244→		if ok {
   245→			matchesSelf = func(addr addrs.ConfigResource) bool {
   246→				// If we know the node is a config resource, we can compare
   247→				// the addresses directly.
   248→				return addr.Equal(self.ResourceAddr())
   249→			}
   250→		}
   251→
   252→		// dedupe addrs when there's multiple instances involved, or
   253→		// multiple paths in the un-reduced graph
   254→		depMap := map[string]addrs.ConfigResource{}
   255→
   256→		// since we need to type-switch over the nodes anyway, we're going to
   257→		// insert the address directly into depMap and forget about the returned
   258→		// set.
   259→		for _, d := range g.Ancestors(v) {
   260→			var addr addrs.ConfigResource
   261→
   262→			switch d := d.(type) {
   263→			case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   264→				instAddr := d.ResourceInstanceAddr()
   265→				addr = instAddr.ContainingResource().Config()
   266→			case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   267→				addr = d.ResourceAddr()
   268→			default:
   269→				continue
   270→			}
   271→
   272→			if matchesSelf(addr) {
   273→				continue
   274→			}
   275→
   276→			depMap[addr.String()] = addr
   277→		}
   278→
   279→		deps := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(depMap))
   280→		for _, d := range depMap {
   281→			deps = append(deps, d)
   282→		}
   283→		sort.Slice(deps, func(i, j int) bool {
   284→			return deps[i].String() < deps[j].String()
   285→		})
   286→
   287→		log.Printf("[TRACE] AttachDependenciesTransformer: %s depends on %s", dag.VertexName(v), deps)
   288→		attacher.AttachDependencies(deps)
   289→	}
   290→
   291→	return nil
   292→}
   293→
   294→func isDependableResource(v dag.Vertex) bool {
   295→	switch v.(type) {
   296→	case GraphNodeResourceInstance:
   297→		return true
   298→	case GraphNodeConfigResource:
   299→		return true
   300→	}

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    15→)
    16→
    17→// ConfigTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds all the resources
    18→// from the configuration to the graph.
    19→//
    20→// The module used to configure this transformer must be the root module.
    21→//
    22→// Only resources are added to the graph. Variables, outputs, and
    23→// providers must be added via other transforms.
    24→//
    25→// Unlike ConfigTransformerOld, this transformer creates a graph with
    26→// all resources including module resources, rather than creating module
    27→// nodes that are then "flattened".
    28→type ConfigTransformer struct {
    29→	Concrete ConcreteResourceNodeFunc
    30→
    31→	// Module is the module to add resources from.
    32→	Config *configs.Config
    33→
    34→	// Mode will only add resources that match the given mode
    35→	ModeFilter bool
    36→	Mode       addrs.ResourceMode
    37→
    38→	// some actions are skipped during the destroy process
    39→	destroy bool
    40→
    41→	// importTargets specifies a slice of addresses that will have state
    42→	// imported for them.
    43→	importTargets []*ImportTarget
    44→
    45→	// generateConfigPathForImportTargets tells the graph where to write any
    46→	// generated config for import targets that are not contained within config.
    47→	//
    48→	// If this is empty and an import target has no config, the graph will
    49→	// simply import the state for the target and any follow-up operations will
    50→	// try to delete the imported resource unless the config is updated
    51→	// manually.
    52→	generateConfigPathForImportTargets string
    53→}
    54→
    55→func (t *ConfigTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    56→	// If no configuration is available, we don't do anything
    57→	if t.Config == nil {
    58→		return nil
    59→	}
    60→
    61→	if err := t.validateImportTargets(); err != nil {
    62→		return err
    63→	}
    64→
    65→	// Start the transformation process
    66→	return t.transform(g, t.Config)
    67→}
    68→
    69→func (t *ConfigTransformer) transform(g *Graph, config *configs.Config) error {
    70→	// If no config, do nothing
    71→	if config == nil {
    72→		return nil
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	// Add our resources
    76→	if err := t.transformSingle(g, config); err != nil {
    77→		return err
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	// Transform all the children without generating config.
    81→	for _, c := range config.Children {
    82→		if err := t.transform(g, c); err != nil {
    83→			return err
    84→		}
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	return nil
    88→}
    89→
    90→func (t *ConfigTransformer) transformSingle(g *Graph, config *configs.Config) error {
    91→	path := config.Path
    92→	module := config.Module
    93→	log.Printf("[TRACE] ConfigTransformer: Starting for path: %v", path)
    94→
    95→	var allResources []*configs.Resource
    96→	if !t.destroy {
    97→		for _, r := range module.ManagedResources {
    98→			allResources = append(allResources, r)
    99→		}
   100→		for _, r := range module.DataResources {
   101→			allResources = append(allResources, r)
   102→		}
   103→	}
   104→
   105→	// ephemeral resources act like temporary values and must be added to the
   106→	// graph even during destroy operations.
   107→	for _, r := range module.EphemeralResources {
   108→		allResources = append(allResources, r)
   109→	}
   110→
   111→	// Take a copy of the import targets, so we can edit them as we go.
   112→	// Only include import targets that are targeting the current module.
   113→	var importTargets []*ImportTarget
   114→	for _, target := range t.importTargets {
   115→		switch {
   116→		case target.Config == nil:
   117→			if target.LegacyAddr.Module.Module().Equal(config.Path) {
   118→				importTargets = append(importTargets, target)
   119→			}
   120→		default:
   121→			if target.Config.ToResource.Module.Equal(config.Path) {
   122→				importTargets = append(importTargets, target)
   123→			}
   124→		}
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	for _, r := range allResources {
   128→		relAddr := r.Addr()
   129→
   130→		if t.ModeFilter && relAddr.Mode != t.Mode {
   131→			// Skip non-matching modes
   132→			continue
   133→		}
   134→
   135→		// If any of the import targets can apply to this node's instances,
   136→		// filter them down to the applicable addresses.
   137→		var imports []*ImportTarget
   138→		configAddr := relAddr.InModule(path)
   139→
   140→		var matchedIndices []int
   141→		for ix, i := range importTargets {
   142→			if i.LegacyAddr.ConfigResource().Equal(configAddr) {
   143→				matchedIndices = append(matchedIndices, ix)
   144→				imports = append(imports, i)
   145→
   146→			}
   147→			if i.Config != nil && i.Config.ToResource.Equal(configAddr) {
   148→				// This import target has been claimed by an actual resource,
   149→				// let's make a note of this to remove it from the targets.
   150→				matchedIndices = append(matchedIndices, ix)
   151→				imports = append(imports, i)
   152→			}
   153→		}
   154→
   155→		for ix := len(matchedIndices) - 1; ix >= 0; ix-- {
   156→			tIx := matchedIndices[ix]
   157→
   158→			// We do this backwards, since it means we don't have to adjust the
   159→			// later indices as we change the length of import targets.
   160→			//
   161→			// We need to do this separately, as a single resource could match
   162→			// multiple import targets.
   163→			importTargets = append(importTargets[:tIx], importTargets[tIx+1:]...)
   164→		}
   165→
   166→		abstract := &NodeAbstractResource{
   167→			Addr: addrs.ConfigResource{
   168→				Resource: relAddr,
   169→				Module:   path,
   170→			},
   171→			importTargets: imports,
   172→		}
   173→
   174→		var node dag.Vertex = abstract
   175→		if f := t.Concrete; f != nil {
   176→			node = f(abstract)
   177→		}
   178→
   179→		g.Add(node)
   180→	}
   181→
   182→	// If any import targets were not claimed by resources we may be
   183→	// generating configuration. Add them to the graph for validation.
   184→	for _, i := range importTargets {
   185→		log.Printf("[DEBUG] ConfigTransformer: adding config generation node for %s", i.Config.ToResource)
   186→
   187→		// TODO: if config generation is ever supported for for_each
   188→		// resources, this will add multiple nodes for the same
   189→		// resource
   190→		abstract := &NodeAbstractResource{
   191→			Addr:               i.Config.ToResource,
   192→			importTargets:      []*ImportTarget{i},
   193→			generateConfigPath: t.generateConfigPathForImportTargets,
   194→		}
   195→		var node dag.Vertex = abstract
   196→		if f := t.Concrete; f != nil {
   197→			node = f(abstract)
   198→		}
   199→
   200→		g.Add(node)

    70→	// If no config, do nothing
    71→	if config == nil {
    72→		return nil
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	// Add our resources
    76→	if err := t.transformSingle(g, config); err != nil {
    77→		return err
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	// Transform all the children without generating config.
    81→	for _, c := range config.Children {
    82→		if err := t.transform(g, c); err != nil {
    83→			return err
    84→		}
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	return nil
    88→}
    89→
    90→func (t *ConfigTransformer) transformSingle(g *Graph, config *configs.Config) error {
    91→	path := config.Path
    92→	module := config.Module
    93→	log.Printf("[TRACE] ConfigTransformer: Starting for path: %v", path)
    94→
    95→	var allResources []*configs.Resource
    96→	if !t.destroy {
    97→		for _, r := range module.ManagedResources {
    98→			allResources = append(allResources, r)
    99→		}
   100→		for _, r := range module.DataResources {
   101→			allResources = append(allResources, r)
   102→		}
   103→	}
   104→
   105→	// ephemeral resources act like temporary values and must be added to the
   106→	// graph even during destroy operations.
   107→	for _, r := range module.EphemeralResources {
   108→		allResources = append(allResources, r)
   109→	}
   110→
   111→	// Take a copy of the import targets, so we can edit them as we go.
   112→	// Only include import targets that are targeting the current module.
   113→	var importTargets []*ImportTarget
   114→	for _, target := range t.importTargets {
   115→		switch {
   116→		case target.Config == nil:
   117→			if target.LegacyAddr.Module.Module().Equal(config.Path) {
   118→				importTargets = append(importTargets, target)
   119→			}
   120→		default:
   121→			if target.Config.ToResource.Module.Equal(config.Path) {
   122→				importTargets = append(importTargets, target)
   123→			}
   124→		}
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	for _, r := range allResources {
   128→		relAddr := r.Addr()
   129→
   130→		if t.ModeFilter && relAddr.Mode != t.Mode {
   131→			// Skip non-matching modes
   132→			continue
   133→		}
   134→
   135→		// If any of the import targets can apply to this node's instances,
   136→		// filter them down to the applicable addresses.
   137→		var imports []*ImportTarget
   138→		configAddr := relAddr.InModule(path)
   139→
   140→		var matchedIndices []int
   141→		for ix, i := range importTargets {
   142→			if i.LegacyAddr.ConfigResource().Equal(configAddr) {
   143→				matchedIndices = append(matchedIndices, ix)
   144→				imports = append(imports, i)
   145→
   146→			}
   147→			if i.Config != nil && i.Config.ToResource.Equal(configAddr) {
   148→				// This import target has been claimed by an actual resource,
   149→				// let's make a note of this to remove it from the targets.
   150→				matchedIndices = append(matchedIndices, ix)
   151→				imports = append(imports, i)
   152→			}
   153→		}
   154→
   155→		for ix := len(matchedIndices) - 1; ix >= 0; ix-- {
   156→			tIx := matchedIndices[ix]

   159→			// later indices as we change the length of import targets.
   160→			//

   162→			// multiple import targets.

   172→		}

   175→		if f := t.Concrete; f != nil {
   176→			node = f(abstract)
   177→		}

   179→		g.Add(node)
   180→	}

   183→	// generating configuration. Add them to the graph for validation.
   184→	for _, i := range importTargets {

   188→		// resources, this will add multiple nodes for the same
   189→		// resource

   192→			importTargets:      []*ImportTarget{i},
   193→			generateConfigPath: t.generateConfigPathForImportTargets,
   194→		}

   196→		if f := t.Concrete; f != nil {

    16→
    17→// ConfigTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds all the resources
    18→// from the configuration to the graph.
    19→//
    20→// The module used to configure this transformer must be the root module.
    21→//
    22→// Only resources are added to the graph. Variables, outputs, and
    23→// providers must be added via other transforms.
    24→//
    25→// Unlike ConfigTransformerOld, this transformer creates a graph with
    26→// all resources including module resources, rather than creating module
    27→// nodes that are then "flattened".
    28→type ConfigTransformer struct {
    29→	Concrete ConcreteResourceNodeFunc
    30→
    31→	// Module is the module to add resources from.
    32→	Config *configs.Config
    33→
    34→	// Mode will only add resources that match the given mode
    35→	ModeFilter bool
    36→	Mode       addrs.ResourceMode
    37→
    38→	// some actions are skipped during the destroy process
    39→	destroy bool
    40→
    41→	// importTargets specifies a slice of addresses that will have state
    42→	// imported for them.
    43→	importTargets []*ImportTarget
    44→
    45→	// generateConfigPathForImportTargets tells the graph where to write any
    46→	// generated config for import targets that are not contained within config.
    47→	//
    48→	// If this is empty and an import target has no config, the graph will
    49→	// simply import the state for the target and any follow-up operations will
    50→	// try to delete the imported resource unless the config is updated
    51→	// manually.
    52→	generateConfigPathForImportTargets string
    53→}
    54→
    55→func (t *ConfigTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    56→	// If no configuration is available, we don't do anything
    57→	if t.Config == nil {
    58→		return nil
    59→	}
    60→
    61→	if err := t.validateImportTargets(); err != nil {
    62→		return err
    63→	}
    64→
    65→	// Start the transformation process
    66→	return t.transform(g, t.Config)
    67→}
    68→
    69→func (t *ConfigTransformer) transform(g *Graph, config *configs.Config) error {
    70→	// If no config, do nothing
    71→	if config == nil {
    72→		return nil
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	// Add our resources
    76→	if err := t.transformSingle(g, config); err != nil {
    77→		return err
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	// Transform all the children without generating config.
    81→	for _, c := range config.Children {
    82→		if err := t.transform(g, c); err != nil {
    83→			return err
    84→		}
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	return nil
    88→}
    89→
    90→func (t *ConfigTransformer) transformSingle(g *Graph, config *configs.Config) error {
    91→	path := config.Path
    92→	module := config.Module
    93→	log.Printf("[TRACE] ConfigTransformer: Starting for path: %v", path)

    96→	if !t.destroy {
    97→		for _, r := range module.ManagedResources {
    98→			allResources = append(allResources, r)
    99→		}
   100→		for _, r := range module.DataResources {
   101→			allResources = append(allResources, r)
   102→		}
   103→	}
   104→
   105→	// ephemeral resources act like temporary values and must be added to the
   106→	// graph even during destroy operations.
   107→	for _, r := range module.EphemeralResources {
   108→		allResources = append(allResources, r)
   109→	}
   110→
   111→	// Take a copy of the import targets, so we can edit them as we go.
   112→	// Only include import targets that are targeting the current module.

   114→	for _, target := range t.importTargets {

   122→				importTargets = append(importTargets, target)
   123→			}
   124→		}
   125→	}
   126→
   127→	for _, r := range allResources {
   128→		relAddr := r.Addr()
   129→
   130→		if t.ModeFilter && relAddr.Mode != t.Mode {
   131→			// Skip non-matching modes
   132→			continue
   133→		}
   134→
   135→		// If any of the import targets can apply to this node's instances,
   136→		// filter them down to the applicable addresses.
   137→		var imports []*ImportTarget
   138→		configAddr := relAddr.InModule(path)
   139→
   140→		var matchedIndices []int
   141→		for ix, i := range importTargets {
   142→			if i.LegacyAddr.ConfigResource().Equal(configAddr) {
   143→				matchedIndices = append(matchedIndices, ix)

   149→				// let's make a note of this to remove it from the targets.
   150→				matchedIndices = append(matchedIndices, ix)
   151→				imports = append(imports, i)
   152→			}
   153→		}
   154→
   155→		for ix := len(matchedIndices) - 1; ix >= 0; ix-- {
   156→			tIx := matchedIndices[ix]
   157→
   158→			// We do this backwards, since it means we don't have to adjust the
   159→			// later indices as we change the length of import targets.
   160→			//

   162→			// multiple import targets.
   163→			importTargets = append(importTargets[:tIx], importTargets[tIx+1:]...)
   164→		}

   166→		abstract := &NodeAbstractResource{

   170→			},
   171→			importTargets: imports,
   172→		}

   174→		var node dag.Vertex = abstract

   180→	}

   183→	// generating configuration. Add them to the graph for validation.
   184→	for _, i := range importTargets {
   185→		log.Printf("[DEBUG] ConfigTransformer: adding config generation node for %s", i.Config.ToResource)
   186→
   187→		// TODO: if config generation is ever supported for for_each
   188→		// resources, this will add multiple nodes for the same
   189→		// resource
   190→		abstract := &NodeAbstractResource{

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"log"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
    11→)
    12→
    13→// GraphNodeAttachResourceState is an interface that can be implemented
    14→// to request that a ResourceState is attached to the node.
    15→//
    16→// Due to a historical naming inconsistency, the type ResourceState actually
    17→// represents the state for a particular _instance_, while InstanceState
    18→// represents the values for that instance during a particular phase
    19→// (e.g. primary vs. deposed). Consequently, GraphNodeAttachResourceState
    20→// is supported only for nodes that represent resource instances, even though
    21→// the name might suggest it is for containing resources.
    22→type GraphNodeAttachResourceState interface {
    23→	GraphNodeResourceInstance
    24→
    25→	// Sets the state
    26→	AttachResourceState(*states.Resource)
    27→}
    28→
    29→// AttachStateTransformer goes through the graph and attaches
    30→// state to nodes that implement the interfaces above.
    31→type AttachStateTransformer struct {
    32→	State *states.State // State is the root state
    33→}
    34→
    35→func (t *AttachStateTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    36→	// If no state, then nothing to do
    37→	if t.State == nil {
    38→		log.Printf("[DEBUG] Not attaching any node states: overall state is nil")
    39→		return nil
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
    43→		// Nodes implement this interface to request state attachment.
    44→		an, ok := v.(GraphNodeAttachResourceState)
    45→		if !ok {
    46→			continue
    47→		}
    48→		addr := an.ResourceInstanceAddr()
    49→
    50→		rs := t.State.Resource(addr.ContainingResource())
    51→		if rs == nil {
    52→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] Resource state not found for node %q, instance %s", dag.VertexName(v), addr)
    53→			continue
    54→		}
    55→
    56→		is := rs.Instance(addr.Resource.Key)
    57→		if is == nil {
    58→			// We don't actually need this here, since we'll attach the whole
    59→			// resource state, but we still check because it'd be weird
    60→			// for the specific instance we're attaching to not to exist.
    61→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] Resource instance state not found for node %q, instance %s", dag.VertexName(v), addr)
    62→			continue
    63→		}
    64→
    65→		// make sure to attach a copy of the state, so instances can modify the
    66→		// same ResourceState.
    67→		an.AttachResourceState(rs.DeepCopy())
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	return nil
    71→}
    72→

    48→		addr := an.ResourceInstanceAddr()
    49→
    50→		rs := t.State.Resource(addr.ContainingResource())
    51→		if rs == nil {
    52→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] Resource state not found for node %q, instance %s", dag.VertexName(v), addr)
    53→			continue
    54→		}
    55→
    56→		is := rs.Instance(addr.Resource.Key)
    57→		if is == nil {
    58→			// We don't actually need this here, since we'll attach the whole
    59→			// resource state, but we still check because it'd be weird
    60→			// for the specific instance we're attaching to not to exist.
    61→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] Resource instance state not found for node %q, instance %s", dag.VertexName(v), addr)
    62→			continue
    63→		}
    64→
    65→		// make sure to attach a copy of the state, so instances can modify the
    66→		// same ResourceState.
    67→		an.AttachResourceState(rs.DeepCopy())
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	return nil
    71→}
    72→

1→// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
     2→// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
     3→
     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2"
    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
    12→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    16→)
    17→
    18→func transformProviders(concrete ConcreteProviderNodeFunc, config *configs.Config, externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface) GraphTransformer {
    19→	return GraphTransformMulti(
    20→		// Add placeholder nodes for any externally-configured providers
    21→		&externalProviderTransformer{
    22→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
    23→		},
    24→		// Add providers from the config
    25→		&ProviderConfigTransformer{
    26→			Config:   config,
    27→			Concrete: concrete,
    28→		},
    29→		// Add any remaining missing providers
    30→		&MissingProviderTransformer{
    31→			Config:   config,
    32→			Concrete: concrete,
    33→		},
    34→		// Connect the providers
    35→		&ProviderTransformer{
    36→			Config: config,
    37→		},
    38→		// Remove unused providers and proxies
    39→		&PruneProviderTransformer{},
    40→	)
    41→}
    42→
    43→// GraphNodeProvider is an interface that nodes that can be a provider
    44→// must implement.
    45→//
    46→// ProviderAddr returns the address of the provider configuration this
    47→// satisfies, which is relative to the path returned by method Path().
    48→//
    49→// Name returns the full name of the provider in the config.
    50→type GraphNodeProvider interface {
    51→	GraphNodeModulePath
    52→	ProviderAddr() addrs.AbsProviderConfig
    53→	Name() string
    54→}
    55→
    56→// GraphNodeCloseProvider is an interface that nodes that can be a close
    57→// provider must implement. The CloseProviderName returned is the name of
    58→// the provider they satisfy.
    59→type GraphNodeCloseProvider interface {
    60→	GraphNodeModulePath
    61→	CloseProviderAddr() addrs.AbsProviderConfig
    62→}
    63→
    64→// GraphNodeProviderConsumer is an interface that nodes that require
    65→// a provider must implement. ProvidedBy must return the address of the provider
    66→// to use, which will be resolved to a configuration either in the same module
    67→// or in an ancestor module, with the resulting absolute address passed to
    68→// SetProvider.
    69→type GraphNodeProviderConsumer interface {
    70→	GraphNodeModulePath
    71→	// ProvidedBy returns the address of the provider configuration the node
    72→	// refers to, if available. The following value types may be returned:
    73→	//
    74→	//   nil + exact true: the node does not require a provider
    75→	// * addrs.LocalProviderConfig: the provider was set in the resource config
    76→	// * addrs.AbsProviderConfig + exact true: the provider configuration was
    77→	//   taken from the instance state.
    78→	// * addrs.AbsProviderConfig + exact false: no config or state; the returned
    79→	//   value is a default provider configuration address for the resource's
    80→	//   Provider
    81→	ProvidedBy() (addr addrs.ProviderConfig, exact bool)
    82→
    83→	// Provider() returns the Provider FQN for the node.
    84→	Provider() (provider addrs.Provider)
    85→
    86→	// Set the resolved provider address for this resource.
    87→	SetProvider(addrs.AbsProviderConfig)
    88→}
    89→
    90→// ProviderTransformer is a GraphTransformer that maps resources to providers
    91→// within the graph. This will error if there are any resources that don't map
    92→// to proper resources.
    93→type ProviderTransformer struct {
    94→	Config *configs.Config
    95→}
    96→
    97→func (t *ProviderTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    98→	// We need to find a provider configuration address for each resource
    99→	// either directly represented by a node or referenced by a node in
   100→	// the graph, and then create graph edges from provider to provider user
   101→	// so that the providers will get initialized first.
   102→
   103→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   104→
   105→	// To start, we'll collect the _requested_ provider addresses for each
   106→	// node, which we'll then resolve (handling provider inheritence, etc) in
   107→	// the next step.
   108→	// Our "requested" map is from graph vertices to string representations of
   109→	// provider config addresses (for deduping) to requests.
   110→	type ProviderRequest struct {
   111→		Addr  addrs.AbsProviderConfig
   112→		Exact bool // If true, inheritence from parent modules is not attempted
   113→	}
   114→	requested := map[dag.Vertex]map[string]ProviderRequest{}
   115→	needConfigured := map[string]addrs.AbsProviderConfig{}
   116→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
   117→		// Does the vertex _directly_ use a provider?
   118→		if pv, ok := v.(GraphNodeProviderConsumer); ok {
   119→			providerAddr, exact := pv.ProvidedBy()
   120→			if providerAddr == nil && exact {
   121→				// no provider is required
   122→				continue
   123→			}
   124→
   125→			requested[v] = make(map[string]ProviderRequest)
   126→
   127→			var absPc addrs.AbsProviderConfig
   128→
   129→			switch p := providerAddr.(type) {
   130→			case addrs.AbsProviderConfig:
   131→				// ProvidedBy() returns an AbsProviderConfig when the provider
   132→				// configuration is set in state, so we do not need to verify
   133→				// the FQN matches.
   134→				absPc = p
   135→
   136→				if exact {
   137→					log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: %s is provided by %s exactly", dag.VertexName(v), absPc)
   138→				}
   139→
   140→			case addrs.LocalProviderConfig:
   141→				// ProvidedBy() return a LocalProviderConfig when the resource
   142→				// contains a `provider` attribute
   143→				absPc.Provider = pv.Provider()
   144→				modPath := pv.ModulePath()
   145→				if t.Config == nil {
   146→					absPc.Module = modPath
   147→					absPc.Alias = p.Alias
   148→					break
   149→				}
   150→
   151→				absPc.Module = modPath
   152→				absPc.Alias = p.Alias
   153→
   154→			default:
   155→				// This should never happen; the case statements are meant to be exhaustive
   156→				panic(fmt.Sprintf("%s: provider for %s couldn't be determined", dag.VertexName(v), absPc))
   157→			}
   158→
   159→			requested[v][absPc.String()] = ProviderRequest{
   160→				Addr:  absPc,
   161→				Exact: exact,
   162→			}
   163→
   164→			// Direct references need the provider configured as well as initialized
   165→			needConfigured[absPc.String()] = absPc
   166→		}
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	// Now we'll go through all the requested addresses we just collected and
   170→	// figure out which _actual_ config address each belongs to, after resolving
   171→	// for provider inheritance and passing.
   172→	m := providerVertexMap(g)
   173→	for v, reqs := range requested {
   174→		for key, req := range reqs {
   175→			p := req.Addr
   176→			target := m[key]
   177→
   178→			_, ok := v.(GraphNodeModulePath)
   179→			if !ok && target == nil {
   180→				// No target and no path to traverse up from
   181→				diags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf("%s: provider %s couldn't be found", dag.VertexName(v), p))
   182→				continue
   183→			}
   184→
   185→			if target != nil {
   186→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: exact match for %s serving %s", p, dag.VertexName(v))
   187→			}
   188→
   189→			// if we don't have a provider at this level, walk up the path looking for one,
   190→			// unless we were told to be exact.
   191→			if target == nil && !req.Exact {
   192→				for pp, ok := p.Inherited(); ok; pp, ok = pp.Inherited() {
   193→					key := pp.String()
   194→					target = m[key]
   195→					if target != nil {
   196→						log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: %s uses inherited configuration %s", dag.VertexName(v), pp)
   197→						break
   198→					}
   199→					log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: looking for %s to serve %s", pp, dag.VertexName(v))
   200→				}
   201→			}
   202→
   203→			// If this provider doesn't need to be configured then we can just
   204→			// stub it out with an init-only provider node, which will just
   205→			// start up the provider and fetch its schema.
   206→			if _, exists := needConfigured[key]; target == nil && !exists {
   207→				stubAddr := addrs.AbsProviderConfig{
   208→					Module:   addrs.RootModule,
   209→					Provider: p.Provider,
   210→				}
   211→				stub := &NodeEvalableProvider{
   212→					&NodeAbstractProvider{
   213→						Addr: stubAddr,
   214→					},
   215→				}
   216→				m[stubAddr.String()] = stub
   217→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: creating init-only node for %s", stubAddr)
   218→				target = stub
   219→				g.Add(target)
   220→			}
   221→
   222→			if target == nil {
   223→				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   224→					tfdiags.Error,
   225→					"Provider configuration not present",
   226→					fmt.Sprintf(
   227→						"To work with %s its original provider configuration at %s is required, but it has been removed. This occurs when a provider configuration is removed while objects created by that provider still exist in the state. Re-add the provider configuration to destroy %s, after which you can remove the provider configuration again.",
   228→						dag.VertexName(v), p, dag.VertexName(v),
   229→					),
   230→				))
   231→				break
   232→			}
   233→
   234→			// see if this is a proxy provider pointing to another concrete config
   235→			if p, ok := target.(*graphNodeProxyProvider); ok {
   236→				g.Remove(p)
   237→				target = p.Target()
   238→			}
   239→
   240→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] ProviderTransformer: %q (%T) needs %s", dag.VertexName(v), v, dag.VertexName(target))
   241→			if pv, ok := v.(GraphNodeProviderConsumer); ok {
   242→				pv.SetProvider(target.ProviderAddr())
   243→			}
   244→			g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(v, target))
   245→		}
   246→	}
   247→
   248→	return diags.Err()
   249→}
   250→
   251→// CloseProviderTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds nodes to the
   252→// graph that will close open provider connections that aren't needed anymore.
   253→// A provider connection is not needed anymore once all depended resources
   254→// in the graph are evaluated.
   255→type CloseProviderTransformer struct{}
   256→
   257→func (t *CloseProviderTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   258→	pm := providerVertexMap(g)
   259→	cpm := make(map[string]*graphNodeCloseProvider)
   260→	var err error
   261→
   262→	for _, p := range pm {
   263→		key := p.ProviderAddr().String()
   264→
   265→		// make sure we haven't created the closer node already
   266→		_, ok := cpm[key]
   267→		if ok {
   268→			log.Printf("[ERROR] CloseProviderTransformer: already created close node for %s", key)
   269→			continue
   270→		}
   271→
   272→		// create a closer for this provider type
   273→		closer := &graphNodeCloseProvider{Addr: p.ProviderAddr()}
   274→		g.Add(closer)
   275→		cpm[key] = closer
   276→
   277→		// Close node depends on the provider itself
   278→		// this is added unconditionally, so it will connect to all instances
   279→		// of the provider. Extra edges will be removed by transitive
   280→		// reduction.
   281→		g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(closer, p))
   282→	}
   283→
   284→	// Now look for all provider consumers and connect them to the appropriate closers.
   285→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
   286→		pc, ok := v.(GraphNodeProviderConsumer)
   287→		if !ok {
   288→			continue
   289→		}
   290→
   291→		p, exact := pc.ProvidedBy()
   292→		if p == nil && exact {
   293→			// this node does not require a provider
   294→			continue
   295→		}
   296→
   297→		provider, ok := p.(addrs.AbsProviderConfig)
   298→		if !ok {
   299→			return fmt.Errorf("%s failed to return a provider reference", dag.VertexName(pc))
   300→		}

    11→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"

    18→func transformProviders(concrete ConcreteProviderNodeFunc, config *configs.Config, externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface) GraphTransformer {
    19→	return GraphTransformMulti(
    20→		// Add placeholder nodes for any externally-configured providers
    21→		&externalProviderTransformer{
    22→			ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
    23→		},
    24→		// Add providers from the config
    25→		&ProviderConfigTransformer{
    26→			Config:   config,
    27→			Concrete: concrete,
    28→		},
    29→		// Add any remaining missing providers
    30→		&MissingProviderTransformer{
    31→			Config:   config,
    32→			Concrete: concrete,
    33→		},

    35→		&ProviderTransformer{
    36→			Config: config,
    37→		},

    43→// GraphNodeProvider is an interface that nodes that can be a provider
    44→// must implement.

    47→// satisfies, which is relative to the path returned by method Path().
    48→//

    50→type GraphNodeProvider interface {
    51→	GraphNodeModulePath
    52→	ProviderAddr() addrs.AbsProviderConfig
    53→	Name() string
    54→}
    55→

    58→// the provider they satisfy.

    62→}
    63→
    64→// GraphNodeProviderConsumer is an interface that nodes that require
    65→// a provider must implement. ProvidedBy must return the address of the provider
    66→// to use, which will be resolved to a configuration either in the same module
    67→// or in an ancestor module, with the resulting absolute address passed to
    68→// SetProvider.

    70→	GraphNodeModulePath
    71→	// ProvidedBy returns the address of the provider configuration the node
    72→	// refers to, if available. The following value types may be returned:
    73→	//
    74→	//   nil + exact true: the node does not require a provider
    75→	// * addrs.LocalProviderConfig: the provider was set in the resource config
    76→	// * addrs.AbsProviderConfig + exact true: the provider configuration was
    77→	//   taken from the instance state.
    78→	// * addrs.AbsProviderConfig + exact false: no config or state; the returned
    79→	//   value is a default provider configuration address for the resource's
    80→	//   Provider

    90→// ProviderTransformer is a GraphTransformer that maps resources to providers
    91→// within the graph. This will error if there are any resources that don't map
    92→// to proper resources.
    93→type ProviderTransformer struct {
    94→	Config *configs.Config

    96→
    97→func (t *ProviderTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
    98→	// We need to find a provider configuration address for each resource
    99→	// either directly represented by a node or referenced by a node in
   100→	// the graph, and then create graph edges from provider to provider user
   101→	// so that the providers will get initialized first.
   102→
   103→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   104→
   105→	// To start, we'll collect the _requested_ provider addresses for each
   106→	// node, which we'll then resolve (handling provider inheritence, etc) in
   107→	// the next step.
   108→	// Our "requested" map is from graph vertices to string representations of
   109→	// provider config addresses (for deduping) to requests.
   110→	type ProviderRequest struct {
   111→		Addr  addrs.AbsProviderConfig
   112→		Exact bool // If true, inheritence from parent modules is not attempted
   113→	}
   114→	requested := map[dag.Vertex]map[string]ProviderRequest{}
   115→	needConfigured := map[string]addrs.AbsProviderConfig{}
   116→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
   117→		// Does the vertex _directly_ use a provider?
   118→		if pv, ok := v.(GraphNodeProviderConsumer); ok {
   119→			providerAddr, exact := pv.ProvidedBy()
   120→			if providerAddr == nil && exact {
   121→				// no provider is required
   122→				continue
   123→			}
   124→
   125→			requested[v] = make(map[string]ProviderRequest)
   126→
   127→			var absPc addrs.AbsProviderConfig
   128→
   129→			switch p := providerAddr.(type) {
   130→			case addrs.AbsProviderConfig:
   131→				// ProvidedBy() returns an AbsProviderConfig when the provider
   132→				// configuration is set in state, so we do not need to verify
   133→				// the FQN matches.
   134→				absPc = p
   135→
   136→				if exact {
   137→					log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: %s is provided by %s exactly", dag.VertexName(v), absPc)
   138→				}
   139→
   140→			case addrs.LocalProviderConfig:
   141→				// ProvidedBy() return a LocalProviderConfig when the resource
   142→				// contains a `provider` attribute
   143→				absPc.Provider = pv.Provider()
   144→				modPath := pv.ModulePath()
   145→				if t.Config == nil {
   146→					absPc.Module = modPath
   147→					absPc.Alias = p.Alias
   148→					break
   149→				}
   150→
   151→				absPc.Module = modPath
   152→				absPc.Alias = p.Alias
   153→
   154→			default:
   155→				// This should never happen; the case statements are meant to be exhaustive
   156→				panic(fmt.Sprintf("%s: provider for %s couldn't be determined", dag.VertexName(v), absPc))
   157→			}
   158→
   159→			requested[v][absPc.String()] = ProviderRequest{
   160→				Addr:  absPc,
   161→				Exact: exact,
   162→			}
   163→
   164→			// Direct references need the provider configured as well as initialized
   165→			needConfigured[absPc.String()] = absPc
   166→		}
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	// Now we'll go through all the requested addresses we just collected and
   170→	// figure out which _actual_ config address each belongs to, after resolving
   171→	// for provider inheritance and passing.
   172→	m := providerVertexMap(g)
   173→	for v, reqs := range requested {
   174→		for key, req := range reqs {
   175→			p := req.Addr
   176→			target := m[key]
   177→
   178→			_, ok := v.(GraphNodeModulePath)
   179→			if !ok && target == nil {
   180→				// No target and no path to traverse up from
   181→				diags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf("%s: provider %s couldn't be found", dag.VertexName(v), p))
   182→				continue
   183→			}
   184→
   185→			if target != nil {
   186→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: exact match for %s serving %s", p, dag.VertexName(v))
   187→			}
   188→
   189→			// if we don't have a provider at this level, walk up the path looking for one,
   190→			// unless we were told to be exact.
   191→			if target == nil && !req.Exact {
   192→				for pp, ok := p.Inherited(); ok; pp, ok = pp.Inherited() {
   193→					key := pp.String()
   194→					target = m[key]
   195→					if target != nil {
   196→						log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: %s uses inherited configuration %s", dag.VertexName(v), pp)
   197→						break
   198→					}
   199→					log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: looking for %s to serve %s", pp, dag.VertexName(v))
   200→				}
   201→			}
   202→
   203→			// If this provider doesn't need to be configured then we can just
   204→			// stub it out with an init-only provider node, which will just
   205→			// start up the provider and fetch its schema.
   206→			if _, exists := needConfigured[key]; target == nil && !exists {
   207→				stubAddr := addrs.AbsProviderConfig{
   208→					Module:   addrs.RootModule,
   209→					Provider: p.Provider,
   210→				}
   211→				stub := &NodeEvalableProvider{
   212→					&NodeAbstractProvider{
   213→						Addr: stubAddr,
   214→					},
   215→				}
   216→				m[stubAddr.String()] = stub
   217→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: creating init-only node for %s", stubAddr)
   218→				target = stub
   219→				g.Add(target)
   220→			}
   221→
   222→			if target == nil {
   223→				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   224→					tfdiags.Error,
   225→					"Provider configuration not present",
   226→					fmt.Sprintf(
   227→						"To work with %s its original provider configuration at %s is required, but it has been removed. This occurs when a provider configuration is removed while objects created by that provider still exist in the state. Re-add the provider configuration to destroy %s, after which you can remove the provider configuration again.",
   228→						dag.VertexName(v), p, dag.VertexName(v),
   229→					),
   230→				))
   231→				break
   232→			}
   233→
   234→			// see if this is a proxy provider pointing to another concrete config
   235→			if p, ok := target.(*graphNodeProxyProvider); ok {
   236→				g.Remove(p)
   237→				target = p.Target()
   238→			}
   239→
   240→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] ProviderTransformer: %q (%T) needs %s", dag.VertexName(v), v, dag.VertexName(target))
   241→			if pv, ok := v.(GraphNodeProviderConsumer); ok {
   242→				pv.SetProvider(target.ProviderAddr())
   243→			}
   244→			g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(v, target))
   245→		}
   246→	}
   247→
   248→	return diags.Err()
   249→}
   250→
   251→// CloseProviderTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds nodes to the
   252→// graph that will close open provider connections that aren't needed anymore.
   253→// A provider connection is not needed anymore once all depended resources
   254→// in the graph are evaluated.
   255→type CloseProviderTransformer struct{}
   256→
   257→func (t *CloseProviderTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   258→	pm := providerVertexMap(g)
   259→	cpm := make(map[string]*graphNodeCloseProvider)
   260→	var err error
   261→
   262→	for _, p := range pm {
   263→		key := p.ProviderAddr().String()
   264→
   265→		// make sure we haven't created the closer node already
   266→		_, ok := cpm[key]
   267→		if ok {
   268→			log.Printf("[ERROR] CloseProviderTransformer: already created close node for %s", key)
   269→			continue
   270→		}
   271→
   272→		// create a closer for this provider type
   273→		closer := &graphNodeCloseProvider{Addr: p.ProviderAddr()}
   274→		g.Add(closer)
   275→		cpm[key] = closer
   276→
   277→		// Close node depends on the provider itself
   278→		// this is added unconditionally, so it will connect to all instances
   279→		// of the provider. Extra edges will be removed by transitive
   280→		// reduction.
   281→		g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(closer, p))
   282→	}
   283→
   284→	// Now look for all provider consumers and connect them to the appropriate closers.
   285→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
   286→		pc, ok := v.(GraphNodeProviderConsumer)
   287→		if !ok {
   288→			continue
   289→		}
   290→
   291→		p, exact := pc.ProvidedBy()
   292→		if p == nil && exact {
   293→			// this node does not require a provider
   294→			continue
   295→		}
   296→
   297→		provider, ok := p.(addrs.AbsProviderConfig)
   298→		if !ok {
   299→			return fmt.Errorf("%s failed to return a provider reference", dag.VertexName(pc))
   300→		}

   183→			}
   184→
   185→			if target != nil {
   186→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: exact match for %s serving %s", p, dag.VertexName(v))
   187→			}
   188→
   189→			// if we don't have a provider at this level, walk up the path looking for one,
   190→			// unless we were told to be exact.
   191→			if target == nil && !req.Exact {
   192→				for pp, ok := p.Inherited(); ok; pp, ok = pp.Inherited() {
   193→					key := pp.String()
   194→					target = m[key]
   195→					if target != nil {
   196→						log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: %s uses inherited configuration %s", dag.VertexName(v), pp)
   197→						break
   198→					}
   199→					log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: looking for %s to serve %s", pp, dag.VertexName(v))
   200→				}
   201→			}
   202→
   203→			// If this provider doesn't need to be configured then we can just
   204→			// stub it out with an init-only provider node, which will just
   205→			// start up the provider and fetch its schema.
   206→			if _, exists := needConfigured[key]; target == nil && !exists {
   207→				stubAddr := addrs.AbsProviderConfig{
   208→					Module:   addrs.RootModule,
   209→					Provider: p.Provider,
   210→				}
   211→				stub := &NodeEvalableProvider{
   212→					&NodeAbstractProvider{
   213→						Addr: stubAddr,
   214→					},
   215→				}
   216→				m[stubAddr.String()] = stub
   217→				log.Printf("[TRACE] ProviderTransformer: creating init-only node for %s", stubAddr)
   218→				target = stub
   219→				g.Add(target)
   220→			}
   221→
   222→			if target == nil {
   223→				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   224→					tfdiags.Error,
   225→					"Provider configuration not present",
   226→					fmt.Sprintf(
   227→						"To work with %s its original provider configuration at %s is required, but it has been removed. This occurs when a provider configuration is removed while objects created by that provider still exist in the state. Re-add the provider configuration to destroy %s, after which you can remove the provider configuration again.",
   228→						dag.VertexName(v), p, dag.VertexName(v),
   229→					),
   230→				))
   231→				break
   232→			}
   233→
   234→			// see if this is a proxy provider pointing to another concrete config
   235→			if p, ok := target.(*graphNodeProxyProvider); ok {
   236→				g.Remove(p)
   237→				target = p.Target()
   238→			}
   239→
   240→			log.Printf("[DEBUG] ProviderTransformer: %q (%T) needs %s", dag.VertexName(v), v, dag.VertexName(target))
   241→			if pv, ok := v.(GraphNodeProviderConsumer); ok {
   242→				pv.SetProvider(target.ProviderAddr())
   243→			}
   244→			g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(v, target))
   245→		}
   246→	}
   247→
   248→	return diags.Err()
   249→}
   250→
   251→// CloseProviderTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds nodes to the
   252→// graph that will close open provider connections that aren't needed anymore.
   253→// A provider connection is not needed anymore once all depended resources
   254→// in the graph are evaluated.
   255→type CloseProviderTransformer struct{}
   256→
   257→func (t *CloseProviderTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {
   258→	pm := providerVertexMap(g)
   259→	cpm := make(map[string]*graphNodeCloseProvider)
   260→	var err error
   261→
   262→	for _, p := range pm {
   263→		key := p.ProviderAddr().String()
   264→
   265→		// make sure we haven't created the closer node already
   266→		_, ok := cpm[key]
   267→		if ok {
   268→			log.Printf("[ERROR] CloseProviderTransformer: already created close node for %s", key)
   269→			continue
   270→		}
   271→
   272→		// create a closer for this provider type
   273→		closer := &graphNodeCloseProvider{Addr: p.ProviderAddr()}
   274→		g.Add(closer)
   275→		cpm[key] = closer
   276→
   277→		// Close node depends on the provider itself
   278→		// this is added unconditionally, so it will connect to all instances
   279→		// of the provider. Extra edges will be removed by transitive
   280→		// reduction.
   281→		g.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(closer, p))
   282→	}
   283→
   284→	// Now look for all provider consumers and connect them to the appropriate closers.
   285→	for _, v := range g.Vertices() {
   286→		pc, ok := v.(GraphNodeProviderConsumer)
   287→		if !ok {
   288→			continue
   289→		}
   290→
   291→		p, exact := pc.ProvidedBy()
   292→		if p == nil && exact {
   293→			// this node does not require a provider
   294→			continue
   295→		}
   296→
   297→		provider, ok := p.(addrs.AbsProviderConfig)
   298→		if !ok {
   299→			return fmt.Errorf("%s failed to return a provider reference", dag.VertexName(pc))
   300→		}

82→
    83→func (c *Context) walk(graph *Graph, operation walkOperation, opts *graphWalkOpts) (*ContextGraphWalker, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    84→	log.Printf("[DEBUG] Starting graph walk: %s", operation.String())
    85→
    86→	walker := c.graphWalker(graph, operation, opts)
    87→
    88→	// Watch for a stop so we can call the provider Stop() API.
    89→	watchStop, watchWait := c.watchStop(walker)
    90→
    91→	// Walk the real graph, this will block until it completes
    92→	diags := graph.Walk(walker)
    93→
    94→	// Close the channel so the watcher stops, and wait for it to return.
    95→	close(watchStop)
    96→	<-watchWait
    97→
    98→	return walker, diags
    99→}
   100→
   101→func (c *Context) graphWalker(graph *Graph, operation walkOperation, opts *graphWalkOpts) *ContextGraphWalker {
   102→	var state *states.SyncState
   103→	var refreshState *states.SyncState
   104→	var prevRunState *states.SyncState
   105→
   106→	// NOTE: None of the SyncState objects must directly wrap opts.InputState,
   107→	// because we use those to mutate the state object and opts.InputState
   108→	// belongs to our caller and thus we must treat it as immutable.
   109→	//
   110→	// To account for that, most of our SyncState values created below end up
   111→	// wrapping a _deep copy_ of opts.InputState instead.
   112→	inputState := opts.InputState
   113→	if inputState == nil {
   114→		// Lots of callers use nil to represent the "empty" case where we've
   115→		// not run Apply yet, so we tolerate that.
   116→		inputState = states.NewState()
   117→	}
   118→
   119→	switch operation {
   120→	case walkValidate:
   121→		// validate should not use any state
   122→		state = states.NewState().SyncWrapper()
   123→
   124→		// validate currently uses the plan graph, so we have to populate the
   125→		// refreshState and the prevRunState.
   126→		refreshState = states.NewState().SyncWrapper()
   127→		prevRunState = states.NewState().SyncWrapper()
   128→
   129→	case walkPlan, walkPlanDestroy, walkImport:
   130→		state = inputState.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper()
   131→		refreshState = inputState.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper()
   132→		prevRunState = inputState.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper()
   133→
   134→		// For both of our new states we'll discard the previous run's
   135→		// check results, since we can still refer to them from the
   136→		// prevRunState object if we need to.
   137→		state.DiscardCheckResults()
   138→		refreshState.DiscardCheckResults()
   139→
   140→	default:
   141→		state = inputState.DeepCopy().SyncWrapper()
   142→		// Only plan-like walks use refreshState and prevRunState
   143→
   144→		// Discard the input state's check results, because we should create
   145→		// a new set as a result of the graph walk.
   146→		state.DiscardCheckResults()
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	changes := opts.Changes
   150→	if changes == nil {
   151→		// Several of our non-plan walks end up sharing codepaths with the
   152→		// plan walk and thus expect to generate planned changes even though
   153→		// we don't care about them. To avoid those crashing, we'll just
   154→		// insert a placeholder changes object which'll get discarded
   155→		// afterwards.
   156→		changes = plans.NewChanges()
   157→	}
   158→
   159→	if opts.Config == nil {
   160→		panic("Context.graphWalker call without Config")
   161→	}
   162→
   163→	checkState := checks.NewState(opts.Config)
   164→	if opts.PlanTimeCheckResults != nil {
   165→		// We'll re-report all of the same objects we determined during the
   166→		// plan phase so that we can repeat the checks during the apply
   167→		// phase to finalize them.
   168→		for _, configElem := range opts.PlanTimeCheckResults.ConfigResults.Elems {
   169→			if configElem.Value.ObjectAddrsKnown() {
   170→				configAddr := configElem.Key
   171→				checkState.ReportCheckableObjects(configAddr, configElem.Value.ObjectResults.Keys())
   172→			}
   173→		}
   174→	}
   175→
   176→	deferred := deferring.NewDeferred(opts.DeferralAllowed)
   177→	if opts.ExternalDependencyDeferred {
   178→		deferred.SetExternalDependencyDeferred()
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	return &ContextGraphWalker{
   182→		Context:                 c,
   183→		State:                   state,
   184→		Config:                  opts.Config,
   185→		RefreshState:            refreshState,
   186→		Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   187→		PrevRunState:            prevRunState,
   188→		Changes:                 changes.SyncWrapper(),
   189→		NamedValues:             namedvals.NewState(),
   190→		EphemeralResources:      ephemeral.NewResources(),
   191→		Deferrals:               deferred,
   192→		Checks:                  checkState,
   193→		InstanceExpander:        instances.NewExpander(opts.Overrides),
   194→		ExternalProviderConfigs: opts.ExternalProviderConfigs,
   195→		MoveResults:             opts.MoveResults,
   196→		Operation:               operation,
   197→		StopContext:             c.runContext,
   198→		PlanTimestamp:           opts.PlanTimeTimestamp,
   199→		providerFuncResults:     opts.ProviderFuncResults,
   200→		Forget:                  opts.Forget,
   201→	}
   202→}
   203→

   165→		// We'll re-report all of the same objects we determined during the
   166→		// plan phase so that we can repeat the checks during the apply
   167→		// phase to finalize them.
   168→		for _, configElem := range opts.PlanTimeCheckResults.ConfigResults.Elems {
   169→			if configElem.Value.ObjectAddrsKnown() {
   170→				configAddr := configElem.Key
   171→				checkState.ReportCheckableObjects(configAddr, configElem.Value.ObjectResults.Keys())
   172→			}
   173→		}
   174→	}
   175→
   176→	deferred := deferring.NewDeferred(opts.DeferralAllowed)
   177→	if opts.ExternalDependencyDeferred {
   178→		deferred.SetExternalDependencyDeferred()
   179→	}
   180→
   181→	return &ContextGraphWalker{
   182→		Context:                 c,
   183→		State:                   state,
   184→		Config:                  opts.Config,
   185→		RefreshState:            refreshState,
   186→		Overrides:               opts.Overrides,
   187→		PrevRunState:            prevRunState,
   188→		Changes:                 changes.SyncWrapper(),
   189→		NamedValues:             namedvals.NewState(),
   190→		EphemeralResources:      ephemeral.NewResources(),
   191→		Deferrals:               deferred,
   192→		Checks:                  checkState,
   193→		InstanceExpander:        instances.NewExpander(opts.Overrides),
   194→		ExternalProviderConfigs: opts.ExternalProviderConfigs,
   195→		MoveResults:             opts.MoveResults,
   196→		Operation:               operation,
   197→		StopContext:             c.runContext,
   198→		PlanTimestamp:           opts.PlanTimeTimestamp,
   199→		providerFuncResults:     opts.ProviderFuncResults,
   200→		Forget:                  opts.Forget,
   201→	}
   202→}
   203→

     4→package terraform
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2"
    11→	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs/configschema"
    14→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
    15→	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// NodeApplyableProvider represents a provider during an apply.
    19→type NodeApplyableProvider struct {
    20→	*NodeAbstractProvider
    21→}
    22→
    23→var (
    24→	_ GraphNodeExecutable = (*NodeApplyableProvider)(nil)
    25→)
    26→
    27→// GraphNodeExecutable
    28→func (n *NodeApplyableProvider) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    29→	_, err := ctx.InitProvider(n.Addr, n.Config)
    30→	diags = diags.Append(err)
    31→	if diags.HasErrors() {
    32→		return diags
    33→	}
    34→	provider, _, err := getProvider(ctx, n.Addr)
    35→	diags = diags.Append(err)
    36→	if diags.HasErrors() {
    37→		return diags
    38→	}
    39→
    40→	switch op {
    41→	case walkValidate:
    42→		log.Printf("[TRACE] NodeApplyableProvider: validating configuration for %s", n.Addr)
    43→		return diags.Append(n.ValidateProvider(ctx, provider))
    44→	case walkPlan, walkPlanDestroy, walkApply, walkDestroy:
    45→		log.Printf("[TRACE] NodeApplyableProvider: configuring %s", n.Addr)
    46→		return diags.Append(n.ConfigureProvider(ctx, provider, false))
    47→	case walkImport:
    48→		log.Printf("[TRACE] NodeApplyableProvider: configuring %s (requiring that configuration is wholly known)", n.Addr)
    49→		return diags.Append(n.ConfigureProvider(ctx, provider, true))
    50→	}
    51→	return diags
    52→}
    53→
    54→func (n *NodeApplyableProvider) ValidateProvider(ctx EvalContext, provider providers.Interface) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    55→
    56→	configBody := buildProviderConfig(ctx, n.Addr, n.ProviderConfig())
    57→
    58→	// if a provider config is empty (only an alias), return early and don't continue
    59→	// validation. validate doesn't need to fully configure the provider itself, so
    60→	// skipping a provider with an implied configuration won't prevent other validation from completing.
    61→	_, noConfigDiags := configBody.Content(&hcl.BodySchema{})
    62→	if !noConfigDiags.HasErrors() {
    63→		return nil
    64→	}
    65→
    66→	schemaResp := provider.GetProviderSchema()
    67→	diags = diags.Append(schemaResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
    68→	if diags.HasErrors() {
    69→		return diags
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	configSchema := schemaResp.Provider.Block
    73→	if configSchema == nil {
    74→		// Should never happen in real code, but often comes up in tests where
    75→		// mock schemas are being used that tend to be incomplete.
    76→		log.Printf("[WARN] ValidateProvider: no config schema is available for %s, so using empty schema", n.Addr)
    77→		configSchema = &configschema.Block{}
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	configVal, _, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(configBody, configSchema, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)
    81→	if evalDiags.HasErrors() {
    82→		return diags.Append(evalDiags)
    83→	}
    84→	diags = diags.Append(evalDiags)
    85→
    86→	// If our config value contains any marked values, ensure those are
    87→	// stripped out before sending this to the provider
    88→	unmarkedConfigVal, _ := configVal.UnmarkDeep()
    89→
    90→	req := providers.ValidateProviderConfigRequest{
    91→		Config: unmarkedConfigVal,
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	validateResp := provider.ValidateProviderConfig(req)
    95→	diags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
    96→
    97→	return diags
    98→}
    99→
   100→// ConfigureProvider configures a provider that is already initialized and retrieved.
   101→// If verifyConfigIsKnown is true, ConfigureProvider will return an error if the
   102→// provider configVal is not wholly known and is meant only for use during import.
   103→func (n *NodeApplyableProvider) ConfigureProvider(ctx EvalContext, provider providers.Interface, verifyConfigIsKnown bool) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   104→	config := n.ProviderConfig()
   105→
   106→	configBody := buildProviderConfig(ctx, n.Addr, config)
   107→
   108→	resp := provider.GetProviderSchema()
   109→	diags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
   110→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   111→		return diags
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	configSchema := resp.Provider.Block
   115→	configVal, configBody, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(configBody, configSchema, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)
   116→	diags = diags.Append(evalDiags)
   117→	if evalDiags.HasErrors() {
   118→		if config == nil {
   119→			// The error messages from the above evaluation will be confusing
   120→			// if there isn't an explicit "provider" block in the configuration.
   121→			// Add some detail to the error message in this case.
   122→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   123→				tfdiags.Error,
   124→				"Invalid provider configuration",
   125→				fmt.Sprintf(providerConfigErr, n.Addr.Provider),
   126→			))
   127→		}
   128→		return diags
   129→	}
   130→
   131→	if verifyConfigIsKnown && !configVal.IsWhollyKnown() {
   132→		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   133→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   134→			Summary:  "Invalid provider configuration",
   135→			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("The configuration for %s depends on values that cannot be determined until apply.", n.Addr),
   136→			Subject:  &config.DeclRange,
   137→		})
   138→		return diags
   139→	}
   140→
   141→	// If our config value contains any marked values, ensure those are
   142→	// stripped out before sending this to the provider
   143→	unmarkedConfigVal, _ := configVal.UnmarkDeep()
   144→
   145→	// Allow the provider to validate and insert any defaults into the full
   146→	// configuration.
   147→	req := providers.ValidateProviderConfigRequest{
   148→		Config: unmarkedConfigVal,
   149→	}
   150→
   151→	// ValidateProviderConfig is only used for validation. We are intentionally
   152→	// ignoring the PreparedConfig field to maintain existing behavior.
   153→	validateResp := provider.ValidateProviderConfig(req)
   154→	diags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
   155→	if diags.HasErrors() && config == nil {
   156→		// If there isn't an explicit "provider" block in the configuration,
   157→		// this error message won't be very clear. Add some detail to the error
   158→		// message in this case.
   159→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   160→			tfdiags.Error,
   161→			"Invalid provider configuration",
   162→			fmt.Sprintf(providerConfigErr, n.Addr.Provider),
   163→		))
   164→	}
   165→
   166→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   167→		return diags
   168→	}
   169→
   170→	// If the provider returns something different, log a warning to help
   171→	// indicate to provider developers that the value is not used.
   172→	preparedCfg := validateResp.PreparedConfig
   173→	if preparedCfg != cty.NilVal && !preparedCfg.IsNull() && !preparedCfg.RawEquals(unmarkedConfigVal) {
   174→		log.Printf("[WARN] ValidateProviderConfig from %q changed the config value, but that value is unused", n.Addr)
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	configDiags := ctx.ConfigureProvider(n.Addr, unmarkedConfigVal)
   178→	diags = diags.Append(configDiags.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
   179→	if diags.HasErrors() && config == nil {
   180→		// If there isn't an explicit "provider" block in the configuration,
   181→		// this error message won't be very clear. Add some detail to the error
   182→		// message in this case.
   183→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   184→			tfdiags.Error,
   185→			"Invalid provider configuration",
   186→			fmt.Sprintf(providerConfigErr, n.Addr.Provider),
   187→		))
   188→	}
   189→	return diags
   190→}
   191→
   192→// nodeExternalProvider is used instead of [NodeApplyableProvider] when an
   193→// already-configured provider instance has been provided by an external caller,
   194→// and therefore we don't need to do anything to get the provider ready to
   195→// use.
   196→type nodeExternalProvider struct {
   197→	*NodeAbstractProvider
   198→}
   199→
   200→var (
   201→	_ GraphNodeExecutable = (*nodeExternalProvider)(nil)
   202→)
   203→
   204→// Execute implements GraphNodeExecutable.
   205→func (n *nodeExternalProvider) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   206→	log.Printf("[TRACE] nodeExternalProvider: using externally-configured instance for %s", n.Addr)
   207→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   208→
   209→	// Due to how the EvalContext provider cache works, we need to just poke
   210→	// this method with our provider address so that a subsequent call
   211→	// to ctx.Provider will return it successfully.
   212→	// In this case the "config" argument is always ignored, so we leave it
   213→	// set to nil to represent that.
   214→	_, err := ctx.InitProvider(n.Addr, nil)
   215→	if err != nil {
   216→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   217→			tfdiags.Error,
   218→			"Failed to initialize externally-configured provider",
   219→			fmt.Sprintf("Despite it having been pre-initialized by an external caller, %s somehow failed to initialize. This is a bug in Terraform.", n.Addr),
   220→		))
   221→	}
   222→
   223→	return diags
   224→}
   225→
   226→const providerConfigErr = `Provider %q requires explicit configuration. Add a provider block to the root module and configure the provider's required arguments as described in the provider documentation.
   227→`
   228→

    59→	// validation. validate doesn't need to fully configure the provider itself, so
    60→	// skipping a provider with an implied configuration won't prevent other validation from completing.
    61→	_, noConfigDiags := configBody.Content(&hcl.BodySchema{})
    62→	if !noConfigDiags.HasErrors() {
    63→		return nil
    64→	}
    65→
    66→	schemaResp := provider.GetProviderSchema()
    67→	diags = diags.Append(schemaResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
    68→	if diags.HasErrors() {
    69→		return diags
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	configSchema := schemaResp.Provider.Block
    73→	if configSchema == nil {
    74→		// Should never happen in real code, but often comes up in tests where
    75→		// mock schemas are being used that tend to be incomplete.
    76→		log.Printf("[WARN] ValidateProvider: no config schema is available for %s, so using empty schema", n.Addr)
    77→		configSchema = &configschema.Block{}
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	configVal, _, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(configBody, configSchema, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)
    81→	if evalDiags.HasErrors() {
    82→		return diags.Append(evalDiags)
    83→	}
    84→	diags = diags.Append(evalDiags)
    85→
    86→	// If our config value contains any marked values, ensure those are
    87→	// stripped out before sending this to the provider
    88→	unmarkedConfigVal, _ := configVal.UnmarkDeep()
    89→
    90→	req := providers.ValidateProviderConfigRequest{
    91→		Config: unmarkedConfigVal,
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	validateResp := provider.ValidateProviderConfig(req)
    95→	diags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
    96→
    97→	return diags
    98→}
    99→
   100→// ConfigureProvider configures a provider that is already initialized and retrieved.
   101→// If verifyConfigIsKnown is true, ConfigureProvider will return an error if the
   102→// provider configVal is not wholly known and is meant only for use during import.
   103→func (n *NodeApplyableProvider) ConfigureProvider(ctx EvalContext, provider providers.Interface, verifyConfigIsKnown bool) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   104→	config := n.ProviderConfig()
   105→
   106→	configBody := buildProviderConfig(ctx, n.Addr, config)
   107→
   108→	resp := provider.GetProviderSchema()
   109→	diags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
   110→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   111→		return diags
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	configSchema := resp.Provider.Block
   115→	configVal, configBody, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(configBody, configSchema, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)
   116→	diags = diags.Append(evalDiags)
   117→	if evalDiags.HasErrors() {
   118→		if config == nil {
   119→			// The error messages from the above evaluation will be confusing
   120→			// if there isn't an explicit "provider" block in the configuration.
   121→			// Add some detail to the error message in this case.
   122→			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   123→				tfdiags.Error,
   124→				"Invalid provider configuration",
   125→				fmt.Sprintf(providerConfigErr, n.Addr.Provider),
   126→			))
   127→		}
   128→		return diags
   129→	}

   133→			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   134→			Summary:  "Invalid provider configuration",

   136→			Subject:  &config.DeclRange,
   137→		})
   138→		return diags
   139→	}

   142→	// stripped out before sending this to the provider

   146→	// configuration.

   148→		Config: unmarkedConfigVal,
   149→	}

   152→	// ignoring the PreparedConfig field to maintain existing behavior.
   153→	validateResp := provider.ValidateProviderConfig(req)

   157→		// this error message won't be very clear. Add some detail to the error
   158→		// message in this case.
   159→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   160→			tfdiags.Error,
   161→			"Invalid provider configuration",
   162→			fmt.Sprintf(providerConfigErr, n.Addr.Provider),
   163→		))
   164→	}
   165→
   166→	if diags.HasErrors() {
   167→		return diags
   168→	}
   169→

   171→	// indicate to provider developers that the value is not used.
   172→	preparedCfg := validateResp.PreparedConfig

   174→		log.Printf("[WARN] ValidateProviderConfig from %q changed the config value, but that value is unused", n.Addr)
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	configDiags := ctx.ConfigureProvider(n.Addr, unmarkedConfigVal)
   178→	diags = diags.Append(configDiags.InConfigBody(configBody, n.Addr.String()))
   179→	if diags.HasErrors() && config == nil {
   180→		// If there isn't an explicit "provider" block in the configuration,
   181→		// this error message won't be very clear. Add some detail to the error
   182→		// message in this case.
   183→		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   184→			tfdiags.Error,
   185→			"Invalid provider configuration",
   186→			fmt.Sprintf(providerConfigErr, n.Addr.Provider),
   187→		))
   188→	}
   189→	return diags
   190→}

   192→// nodeExternalProvider is used instead of [NodeApplyableProvider] when an
   193→// already-configured provider instance has been provided by an external caller,
   194→// and therefore we don't need to do anything to get the provider ready to
   195→// use.

   197→	*NodeAbstractProvider
   198→}

   201→	_ GraphNodeExecutable = (*nodeExternalProvider)(nil)
   202→)
   203→
   204→// Execute implements GraphNodeExecutable.
   205→func (n *nodeExternalProvider) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   206→	log.Printf("[TRACE] nodeExternalProvider: using externally-configured instance for %s", n.Addr)
   207→	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   208→
   209→	// Due to how the EvalContext provider cache works, we need to just poke

   213→	// set to nil to represent that.

   217→			tfdiags.Error,
   218→			"Failed to initialize externally-configured provider",
   219→			fmt.Sprintf("Despite it having been pre-initialized by an external caller, %s somehow failed to initialize. This is a bug in Terraform.", n.Addr),
   220→		))
   221→	}
   222→
   223→	return diags
   224→}
